text.en: Use EC2 Image Builder to create an Amazon Machine Image (AMI). Install all the patches and agents that are needed to manage and run the application. Update the Auto Scaling group launch configuration to use the AMI.
text.ja: EC2 Image Builder を使用して Amazon Machine Image (AMI) を作成する。アプリケーションを管理・実行するために必要なすべてのパッチとエージェントをインストールする。Auto Scaling グループの起動設定をその AMI を使用するように更新する。

text.en: Use EC2 Image Builder to create an Amazon Machine Image (AMI). Install the latest version of the application and all the patches and agents that are needed to manage and run the application. Update the Auto Scaling group launch configuration to use the AMI.
text.ja: EC2 Image Builder を使用して Amazon Machine Image (AMI) を作成する。アプリケーションの最新バージョン、およびアプリケーションを管理・実行するために必要なすべてのパッチとエージェントをインストールする。Auto Scaling グループの起動設定をその AMI を使用するように更新する。

text.en: Set up AWS CodeDeploy to deploy the most recent version of the application at runtime.
text.ja: AWS CodeDeploy を設定して、実行時にアプリケーションの最新バージョンをデプロイする。

text.en: Set up AWS CodePipeline to deploy the most recent version of the application at runtime.
text.ja: AWS CodePipeline を設定して、実行時にアプリケーションの最新バージョンをデプロイする。

text.en: Remove any commands that perform operating system patching from the UserData script.
text.ja: UserData スクリプトから、OSパッチ適用を行うコマンドを削除する。

comment.en: Why choose A over B? Problem is that B will tie an AMI with a specific version, so if there is a new version, we need to create a new AMI, and that contradicts with "minimize the number of images that are created". Then E over C, D? E is obviously complementary to A, where removing commands from User Data will make the instance booting process much faster (and of course with A you don't need that anymore). C and D also works but 1/not complementary with any other options; 2/CodeDeploy takes time to execute. Hope this helps somebody struggling with this question.

Option E, which suggests removing operating system patching from the UserData script, might reduce the startup time. But this could leave your instances unpatched and vulnerable, which doesn't meet the requirement to apply all available security updates.
The most practical answers
The solution must make the most recent version of the application available at all times
And what about this requisit? "The solution must make the most recent version of the application available at all times". Only the Answer B fulfill this part.

The requirements are: 1. Decreasing the time it takes for EC2 instances to become available during scale-out events. 2. Ensuring the most recent version of the application is available. 3. Applying all available security updates. 4. Minimising the number of images created. [A] will satisfy requirements 1, 3, 4 [B] is similar to A, but will involve more AMI images [C] Since the applications are on EC2 instances, CodeDeploy will do just fine to update the applications to the most recent version [E] Removing any command for updates will leave our instances susceptible to vulnerabilities. Some commands can be removed, leaving the essential ones
well if u choose B and E then this will resolve as well

B) Eliminated - Similar to option A, but also includes the latest version of the application in the AMI. While this reduces initialization time, it violates the requirement to "minimize the number of images" because it necessitates frequent AMI updates to keep the application version current. C) CodeDeploy ensures that the most recent version of the application is deployed during runtime. E) Eliminated - This violates the requirement to "apply all available security updates"

B would require new images for each new app version. Idea is to minimize image creation, so A is a better fit. E does speed up the process, BUT does not cover the app version requirement, nor the necessity to validate images, which codedeploy covers.

The solution must make the most recent version of the application available at all times. B doesn't make sense because "latest version at the time AMI is created" becomes outdated when a newer one comes. C is obviously needed to make the actual "latest" version deploy.
B is faster than A. E delegates all run time to AMI build time on B option.
thanksss a lott!

question.en:
A developer is creating an AWS Lambda function that needs credentials to connect to an Amazon RDS for MySQL database. An Amazon S3 bucket currently stores the credentials. The developer needs to improve the existing solution by implementing credential rotation and secure storage. The developer also needs to provide integration with the Lambda function.
Which solution should the developer use to store and retrieve the credentials with the LEAST management overhead?
question.ja:
あるデベロッパーが、Amazon RDS for MySQL データベースに接続するための認証情報を必要とする AWS Lambda 関数を作成しています。現在、その認証情報は Amazon S3 バケットに保存されています。デベロッパーは、認証情報のローテーションと安全な保存を実装して、既存のソリューションを改善する必要があります。また、Lambda 関数との統合も提供する必要があります。
管理オーバーヘッドを最小限に抑えて認証情報を保存・取得するには、デベロッパーはどの解決策を使用すべきですか。

text.en: Store the credentials in AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store. Select the database that the parameter will access. Use the default AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) key to encrypt the parameter. Enable automatic rotation for the parameter. Use the parameter from Parameter Store on the Lambda function to connect to the database.
text.ja: 認証情報を AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store に保存する。パラメータがアクセスするデータベースを選択する。デフォルトの AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) キーを使用してパラメータを暗号化する。パラメータの自動ローテーションを有効にする。Lambda 関数上で Parameter Store のパラメータを使用してデータベースに接続する。

text.en: Encrypt the credentials with the default AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) key. Store the credentials as environment variables for the Lambda function. Create a second Lambda function to generate new credentials and to rotate the credentials by updating the environment variables of the first Lambda function. Invoke the second Lambda function by using an Amazon EventBridge rule that runs on a schedule. Update the database to use the new credentials. On the first Lambda function, retrieve the credentials from the environment variables. Decrypt the credentials by using AWS KMS, Connect to the database.
text.ja: デフォルトの AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) キーで認証情報を暗号化する。認証情報を Lambda 関数の環境変数として保存する。新しい認証情報を生成し、1つ目の Lambda 関数の環境変数を更新して認証情報をローテーションする2つ目の Lambda 関数を作成する。スケジュールで実行する Amazon EventBridge ルールを使用して、2つ目の Lambda 関数を呼び出す。新しい認証情報を使用するようにデータベースを更新する。1つ目の Lambda 関数上で、環境変数から認証情報を取得する。AWS KMS を使用して認証情報を復号する。データベースに接続する。

text.en: Store the credentials in AWS Secrets Manager. Set the secret type to Credentials for Amazon RDS database. Select the database that the secret will access. Use the default AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) key to encrypt the secret. Enable automatic rotation for the secret. Use the secret from Secrets Manager on the Lambda function to connect to the database.
text.ja: 認証情報を AWS Secrets Manager に保存する。シークレットタイプを「Credentials for Amazon RDS database」に設定する。シークレットがアクセスするデータベースを選択する。デフォルトの AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) キーを使用してシークレットを暗号化する。シークレットの自動ローテーションを有効にする。Lambda 関数上で Secrets Manager のシークレットを使用してデータベースに接続する。

text.en: Encrypt the credentials by using AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS). Store the credentials in an Amazon DynamoDB table. Create a second Lambda function to rotate the credentials. Invoke the second Lambda function by using an Amazon EventBridge rule that runs on a schedule. Update the DynamoDB table. Update the database to use the generated credentials. Retrieve the credentials from DynamoDB with the first Lambda function. Connect to the database.
text.ja: AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) を使用して認証情報を暗号化する。認証情報を Amazon DynamoDB テーブルに保存する。認証情報をローテーションする2つ目の Lambda 関数を作成する。スケジュールで実行する Amazon EventBridge ルールを使用して、2つ目の Lambda 関数を呼び出す。DynamoDB テーブルを更新する。生成された認証情報を使用するようにデータベースを更新する。1つ目の Lambda 関数で DynamoDB から認証情報を取得する。データベースに接続する。

comment.en: C https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/intro.html https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/create_database_secret.html https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/retrieving-secrets_lambda.html

A - KMS provides automatic rotation for AWS Managed Keys, but Custom Managed Keys are not provided (needs to implement rotation logic by dev). A did not specify which key the dev should use. ANW : C - SM provides automatic rotation lambda for RDS Param rotation.
C. "credential rotation" = Security Manager

C This solution minimizes management overhead by leveraging the built-in capabilities of AWS Secrets Manager, such as encryption, automatic rotation, and integration with AWS Lambda. It provides a secure and efficient way to store and retrieve https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/intro.html https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/create_database_secret.html https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/retrieving-secrets_lambda.html
"automatic rotation" "cross region" - Security Manager

A) Eliminated - automatic credential rotation is limited C) Correct - Secrets Manager is specifically designed for securely storing and retrieving secrets. Automatic rotation of credentials is fully supported
C The keyword automatic rotation for the parameter.
C is the correct answer.
Option C. Keyword: Implementing credential rotation and secure storage.

question.en:
A developer has written the following IAM policy to provide access to an Amazon S3 bucket:

Which access does the policy allow regarding the s3:GetObject and s3:PutObject actions?
question.ja:
あるデベロッパーが、Amazon S3 バケットへのアクセスを提供するために次の IAM ポリシーを作成しました。

s3:GetObject および s3:PutObject アクションに関して、このポリシーはどのアクセスを許可していますか。

text.en: Access on all buckets except the “DOC-EXAMPLE-BUCKET” bucket
text.ja: 「DOC-EXAMPLE-BUCKET」バケットを除く、すべてのバケットへのアクセス

text.en: Access on all buckets that start with “DOC-EXAMPLE-BUCKET” except the “DOC-EXAMPLE-BUCKET/secrets” bucket
text.ja: 「DOC-EXAMPLE-BUCKET」で始まるすべてのバケットへのアクセス。ただし「DOC-EXAMPLE-BUCKET/secrets」バケットは除く

text.en: Access on all objects in the “DOC-EXAMPLE-BUCKET” bucket along with access to all S3 actions for objects in the “DOC-EXAMPLE-BUCKET” bucket that start with “secrets”
text.ja: 「DOC-EXAMPLE-BUCKET」バケット内のすべてのオブジェクトへのアクセスに加え、「DOC-EXAMPLE-BUCKET」バケット内で「secrets」で始まるオブジェクトに対するすべての S3 アクションへのアクセス

text.en: Access on all objects in the “DOC-EXAMPLE-BUCKET” bucket except on objects that start with “secrets”
text.ja: 「DOC-EXAMPLE-BUCKET」バケット内のすべてのオブジェクトへのアクセス。ただし「secrets」で始まるオブジェクトは除く

comment.en: D https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/using-with-s3-actions.html
D https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/using-with-s3-actions.html
The condition restricts access to objects that start with the prefix secrets
Me toco en el examen
D is the correct answer.

question.en:
A developer is creating a mobile app that calls a backend service by using an Amazon API Gateway REST API. For integration testing during the development phase, the developer wants to simulate different backend responses without invoking the backend service.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
question.ja:
あるデベロッパーが、Amazon API Gateway REST API を使用してバックエンドサービスを呼び出すモバイルアプリを作成しています。開発段階での統合テストのために、デベロッパーはバックエンドサービスを実際に呼び出さずに異なるバックエンドの応答をシミュレートしたいと考えています。
運用上のオーバーヘッドを最小限に抑えてこの要件を満たす解決策はどれですか。

text.en: Create an AWS Lambda function. Use API Gateway proxy integration to return constant HTTP responses.
text.ja: AWS Lambda 関数を作成する。API Gateway のプロキシ統合を使用して、一定の HTTP レスポンスを返す。

text.en: Create an Amazon EC2 instance that serves the backend REST API by using an AWS CloudFormation template.
text.ja: AWS CloudFormation テンプレートを使用して、バックエンドの REST API を提供する Amazon EC2 インスタンスを作成する。

text.en: Customize the API Gateway stage to select a response type based on the request.
text.ja: リクエストに基づいてレスポンスタイプを選択するように API Gateway のステージをカスタマイズする。

text.en: Use a request mapping template to select the mock integration response.
text.ja: リクエストマッピングテンプレートを使用して、モック統合のレスポンスを選択する。

comment.en: D https://docs.aws.amazon.com/apigateway/latest/developerguide/how-to-mock-integration.html
Chatgpt said D

D. Use a request mapping template to select the mock integration response. Option D allows you to use a request mapping template in API Gateway to select the mock integration response. This approach allows you to simulate different backend responses without invoking the actual backend service. It provides flexibility and control over the responses without the need for additional AWS resources like Lambda functions or EC2 instances, thus minimizing operational overhead.
I'm going with D as well.
C) Eliminated - Stages are not designed for selecting mock responses dynamically. D) Correct - API Gateway provides a mock integration feature where request mapping templates can simulate responses.
without invoking backend service -> mock
This appear at 17 Jun exam
D is the correct answer.

D as per doc https://docs.aws.amazon.com/apigateway/latest/developerguide/how-to-mock-integration.html Wording confused me a bit, with mapping template you do not "select" a response, instead you actually craft it in this case
it's D

question.en:
An Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose delivery stream is receiving customer data that contains personally identifiable information. A developer needs to remove pattern-based customer identifiers from the data and store the modified data in an Amazon S3 bucket.
What should the developer do to meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose 配信ストリームが、個人を特定できる情報を含む顧客データを受信しています。デベロッパーは、データからパターンに基づく顧客識別子を削除し、変更後のデータを Amazon S3 バケットに保存する必要があります。
この要件を満たすために、デベロッパーは何をすべきですか。

text.en: Implement Kinesis Data Firehose data transformation as an AWS Lambda function. Configure the function to remove the customer identifiers. Set an Amazon S3 bucket as the destination of the delivery stream.
text.ja: Kinesis Data Firehose のデータ変換を AWS Lambda 関数として実装する。顧客識別子を削除するように関数を設定する。配信ストリームの送信先として Amazon S3 バケットを設定する。

text.en: Launch an Amazon EC2 instance. Set the EC2 instance as the destination of the delivery stream. Run an application on the EC2 instance to remove the customer identifiers. Store the transformed data in an Amazon S3 bucket.
text.ja: Amazon EC2 インスタンスを起動する。EC2 インスタンスを配信ストリームの送信先として設定する。EC2 インスタンス上でアプリケーションを実行して顧客識別子を削除する。変換後のデータを Amazon S3 バケットに保存する。

text.en: Create an Amazon OpenSearch Service instance. Set the OpenSearch Service instance as the destination of the delivery stream. Use search and replace to remove the customer identifiers. Export the data to an Amazon S3 bucket.
text.ja: Amazon OpenSearch Service インスタンスを作成する。OpenSearch Service インスタンスを配信ストリームの送信先として設定する。検索と置換を使用して顧客識別子を削除する。データを Amazon S3 バケットにエクスポートする。

text.en: Create an AWS Step Functions workflow to remove the customer identifiers. As the last step in the workflow, store the transformed data in an Amazon S3 bucket. Set the workflow as the destination of the delivery stream.
text.ja: 顧客識別子を削除する AWS Step Functions ワークフローを作成する。ワークフローの最後のステップとして、変換後のデータを Amazon S3 バケットに保存する。ワークフローを配信ストリームの送信先として設定する。

comment.en: A https://docs.aws.amazon.com/firehose/latest/dev/data-transformation.html
Kinesis Data Firehose supports the use of an AWS Lambda function for data transformation before delivering the data to a destination
It supports custom data transformation using AWS Lambda

A) Correct - AWS Firehose supports Lambda transforms natively. This is scalable, cost-effective, and integrates seamlessly to remove PII before storing in S3. B) Incorrect - EC2 adds cost, maintenance, and complexity. Not suitable for a real-time, serverless transformation workflow. C) Incorrect - OpenSearch is not designed for stream processing or PII removal. It’s primarily for search and analytics, not transformation. D) Incorrect - Step Functions are not supported as a Firehose destination, and this adds unnecessary orchestration overhead.
A is the correct answer.

question.en:
A developer is using an AWS Lambda function to generate avatars for profile pictures that are uploaded to an Amazon S3 bucket. The Lambda function is automatically invoked for profile pictures that are saved under the /original/ S3 prefix. The developer notices that some pictures cause the Lambda function to time out. The developer wants to implement a fallback mechanism by using another Lambda function that resizes the profile picture.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST development effort?
question.ja:
あるデベロッパーが、Amazon S3 バケットにアップロードされたプロフィール画像のアバターを生成するために AWS Lambda 関数を使用しています。この Lambda 関数は、/original/ という S3 プレフィックスの下に保存されたプロフィール画像に対して自動的に呼び出されます。デベロッパーは、一部の画像で Lambda 関数がタイムアウトすることに気づきました。デベロッパーは、プロフィール画像をリサイズする別の Lambda 関数を使用したフォールバックメカニズムを実装したいと考えています。
開発の手間を最小限に抑えてこの要件を満たす解決策はどれですか。

text.en: Set the image resize Lambda function as a destination of the avatar generator Lambda function for the events that fail processing.
text.ja: 処理に失敗したイベントについて、アバター生成 Lambda 関数の送信先(destination)として画像リサイズ Lambda 関数を設定する。

text.en: Create an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. Set the SQS queue as a destination with an on failure condition for the avatar generator Lambda function. Configure the image resize Lambda function to poll from the SQS queue.
text.ja: Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) キューを作成する。アバター生成 Lambda 関数の失敗時条件(on failure condition)付きの送信先として SQS キューを設定する。画像リサイズ Lambda 関数を SQS キューからポーリングするように設定する。

text.en: Create an AWS Step Functions state machine that invokes the avatar generator Lambda function and uses the image resize Lambda function as a fallback. Create an Amazon EventBridge rule that matches events from the S3 bucket to invoke the state machine.
text.ja: アバター生成 Lambda 関数を呼び出し、画像リサイズ Lambda 関数をフォールバックとして使用する AWS Step Functions ステートマシンを作成する。S3 バケットからのイベントに一致してステートマシンを呼び出す Amazon EventBridge ルールを作成する。

text.en: Create an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic. Set the SNS topic as a destination with an on failure condition for the avatar generator Lambda function. Subscribe the image resize Lambda function to the SNS topic.
text.ja: Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) トピックを作成する。アバター生成 Lambda 関数の失敗時条件(on failure condition)付きの送信先として SNS トピックを設定する。画像リサイズ Lambda 関数を SNS トピックにサブスクライブさせる。

comment.en: Wouldn't A be the Least Effort

Previously, you needed to write the SQS/SNS/EventBridge handling code within your Lambda function and manage retries and failures yourself. With Destinations, you can route asynchronous function results as an execution record to a destination resource without writing additional code. https://aws.amazon.com/ru/blogs/compute/introducing-aws-lambda-destinations/

Option B is the right answer. Can someone say why B cannot be the right answer for this question? Option A fails when there are huge amounts of requests coming to the lambda functions. There is every chance for lambda to throw ProvisionedThrougputExceeded Exception because of the throttling issues. Which is almost the similar reason why Option C will also fail at some point. However, you could use SNS but it is not the best solution. Definitely Option B.

AWS Lambda supports Destinations for asynchronous invocations. You can configure a failure destination that triggers a fallback mechanism, such as invoking another Lambda function. This removes the need for additional services like SQS, SNS, or Step Functions, simplifying the architecture.
Least development effort no emphasis on orchestration

I agree with B because I am considering scalability in my mind if we have thousands/millions of requests at the same time. because of the quota limit, the lambda can fail if we continuously call two functions (step function) together, which may result in another function doing a throttling issue. If we pass the message to the SQS, our function will never face this issue with throttling. and since the question asks us to do the least development efforts. Separation of concerns will make development easier.
A is the correct Answer if we read the question in the last part "The developer wants to implement a fallback mechanism by using another Lambda function"

B is right. A.wrong because Lambda Function Chaining: While Lambda function chaining is possible, it would require modifying the avatar generation Lambda function to include the resize function as a destination for failed events. This might involve additional coding and potentially more complex error handling within the Lambda function.
With AWS Lambda destinations you don't need to interact with code to change this
https://aws.amazon.com/ru/blogs/compute/introducing-aws-lambda-destinations/ this link justifies the answer

The article says "For each execution status such as Success or Failure you can choose one of four destinations: another Lambda function, SNS, SQS, or EventBridge." It hink for this reason the correct one is B.
I agree with the explanation for option B. Scalability is the key

question.en:
A developer needs to migrate an online retail application to AWS to handle an anticipated increase in traffic. The application currently runs on two servers: one server for the web application and another server for the database. The web server renders webpages and manages session state in memory. The database server hosts a MySQL database that contains order details. When traffic to the application is heavy, the memory usage for the web server approaches 100% and the application slows down considerably.
The developer has found that most of the memory increase and performance decrease is related to the load of managing additional user sessions. For the web server migration, the developer will use Amazon EC2 instances with an Auto Scaling group behind an Application Load Balancer.
Which additional set of changes should the developer make to the application to improve the application's performance?
question.ja:
あるデベロッパーが、トラフィックの増加が見込まれるオンライン小売アプリケーションを AWS に移行する必要があります。このアプリケーションは現在、2台のサーバーで稼働しています。1台はWebアプリケーション用、もう1台はデータベース用です。Webサーバーはウェブページをレンダリングし、セッション状態をメモリ内で管理します。データベースサーバーは、注文の詳細を含む MySQL データベースをホストしています。トラフィックが多いとき、Webサーバーのメモリ使用率は100%近くになり、アプリケーションの速度が大幅に低下します。
デベロッパーは、メモリ増加とパフォーマンス低下の大部分が、追加のユーザーセッション管理の負荷に関連していることを見つけました。Webサーバーの移行には、デベロッパーは Application Load Balancer の背後にある Auto Scaling グループを持つ Amazon EC2 インスタンスを使用します。
アプリケーションのパフォーマンスを改善するために、デベロッパーはどの追加の変更を行うべきですか。

text.en: Use an EC2 instance to host the MySQL database. Store the session data and the application data in the MySQL database.
text.ja: EC2 インスタンスを使用して MySQL データベースをホストする。セッションデータとアプリケーションデータの両方を MySQL データベースに保存する。

text.en: Use Amazon ElastiCache for Memcached to store and manage the session data. Use an Amazon RDS for MySQL DB instance to store the application data.
text.ja: Amazon ElastiCache for Memcached を使用してセッションデータを保存・管理する。Amazon RDS for MySQL DB インスタンスを使用してアプリケーションデータを保存する。

text.en: Use Amazon ElastiCache for Memcached to store and manage the session data and the application data.
text.ja: Amazon ElastiCache for Memcached を使用して、セッションデータとアプリケーションデータの両方を保存・管理する。

text.en: Use the EC2 instance store to manage the session data. Use an Amazon RDS for MySQL DB instance to store the application data.
text.ja: EC2 インスタンスストアを使用してセッションデータを管理する。Amazon RDS for MySQL DB インスタンスを使用してアプリケーションデータを保存する。

comment.en: B Session stores are easy to create with Amazon ElastiCache for Memcached. https://aws.amazon.com/elasticache/memcached/ With Amazon RDS, you can deploy scalable MySQL servers in minutes with cost-efficient and resizable hardware capacity. https://aws.amazon.com/rds/mysql/
Option B , how can you image using an EC2 as cache ....
B it is

Session Store is one of the main use case for ElastiCache for Memcached as pwe AWS website https://aws.amazon.com/elasticache/memcached/#:~:text=ElastiCache%20for%20Memcached.-,Session%20Store,-Session%20stores%20are

B) - in-memory data store for managing session state C) Eliminated - ElastiCache is not a suitable replacement for a relational database like MySQL when dealing with structured data and complex queries.
B is the correct answer.
The additional requirement for the faster retrieval of data
B is correct

I choose A. It says that the most of the memory increase is related to the load of managing additional user sessions. So I think Memcached doesn't make sense. Also, isn't bad practice to store session information in db.

question.en:
An application uses Lambda functions to extract metadata from files uploaded to an S3 bucket; the metadata is stored in Amazon DynamoDB. The application starts behaving unexpectedly, and the developer wants to examine the logs of the Lambda function code for errors.
Based on this system configuration, where would the developer find the logs?
question.ja:
あるアプリケーションは、S3 バケットにアップロードされたファイルからメタデータを抽出するために Lambda 関数を使用しており、そのメタデータは Amazon DynamoDB に保存されます。アプリケーションが予期しない動作をするようになり、デベロッパーはエラーを調査するために Lambda 関数のコードのログを確認したいと考えています。
このシステム構成に基づくと、デベロッパーはどこでログを見つけることができますか。

text.en: Amazon S3
text.ja: Amazon S3

text.en: AWS CloudTrail
text.ja: AWS CloudTrail

text.en: Amazon CloudWatch
text.ja: Amazon CloudWatch

text.en: Amazon DynamoDB
text.ja: Amazon DynamoDB

comment.en: C https://docs.aws.amazon.com/prescriptive-guidance/latest/implementing-logging-monitoring-cloudwatch/lambda-logging-metrics.html
This appear at 17 Jun exam

C) - AWS Lambda automatically sends logs generated by your function to Amazon CloudWatch Logs. B) Eliminated - CloudTrail logs API activity and management operations across AWS, such as who invoked the Lambda function. However, it does not provide the function's execution logs.
Answer is C
C is the correct answer.

question.en:
A company needs to harden its container images before the images are in a running state. The company's application uses Amazon Elastic Container Registry (Amazon ECR) as an image registry. Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) for compute, and an AWS CodePipeline pipeline that orchestrates a continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) workflow.
Dynamic application security testing occurs in the final stage of the pipeline after a new image is deployed to a development namespace in the EKS cluster. A developer needs to place an analysis stage before this deployment to analyze the container image earlier in the CI/CD pipeline.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the MOST operational efficiency?
question.ja:
ある会社が、コンテナイメージが実行状態になる前にイメージを強化(harden)する必要があります。この会社のアプリケーションは、イメージレジストリとして Amazon Elastic Container Registry (Amazon ECR) を、コンピューティングとして Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) を、そして継続的インテグレーション・継続的デリバリー(CI/CD)ワークフローを統括する AWS CodePipeline パイプラインを使用しています。
動的アプリケーションセキュリティテストは、新しいイメージが EKS クラスターの development 名前空間にデプロイされた後、パイプラインの最終段階で実行されます。デベロッパーは、この CI/CD パイプラインのより早い段階でコンテナイメージを分析するために、このデプロイの前に分析ステージを配置する必要があります。
運用効率を最も高めてこの要件を満たす解決策はどれですか。

text.en: Build the container image and run the docker scan command locally. Mitigate any findings before pushing changes to the source code repository. Write a pre-commit hook that enforces the use of this workflow before commit.
text.ja: コンテナイメージをビルドし、ローカルで docker scan コマンドを実行する。ソースコードリポジトリに変更をプッシュする前に、検出された問題を修正する。この手順の使用をコミット前に強制する pre-commit フックを作成する。

text.en: Create a new CodePipeline stage that occurs after the container image is built. Configure ECR basic image scanning to scan on image push. Use an AWS Lambda function as the action provider. Configure the Lambda function to check the scan results and to fail the pipeline if there are findings.
text.ja: コンテナイメージのビルド後に実行される新しい CodePipeline ステージを作成する。イメージのプッシュ時にスキャンするように ECR の基本イメージスキャンを設定する。アクションプロバイダーとして AWS Lambda 関数を使用する。スキャン結果を確認し、検出があればパイプラインを失敗させるように Lambda 関数を設定する。

text.en: Create a new CodePipeline stage that occurs after source code has been retrieved from its repository. Run a security scanner on the latest revision of the source code. Fail the pipeline if there are findings.
text.ja: リポジトリからソースコードが取得された後に実行される新しい CodePipeline ステージを作成する。ソースコードの最新リビジョンに対してセキュリティスキャナーを実行する。検出があればパイプラインを失敗させる。

text.en: Add an action to the deployment stage of the pipeline so that the action occurs before the deployment to the EKS cluster. Configure ECR basic image scanning to scan on image push. Use an AWS Lambda function as the action provider. Configure the Lambda function to check the scan results and to fail the pipeline if there are findings.
text.ja: EKS クラスターへのデプロイの前に実行されるように、パイプラインのデプロイステージにアクションを追加する。イメージのプッシュ時にスキャンするように ECR の基本イメージスキャンを設定する。アクションプロバイダーとして AWS Lambda 関数を使用する。スキャン結果を確認し、検出があればパイプラインを失敗させるように Lambda 関数を設定する。

comment.en: B https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECR/latest/userguide/image-scanning-basic.html The below blog post refers to the solution using Amazon Inspector and ECS, but the architecture is almost same as required in this scenario. The built in image scanning in Amazon ECR provides a simpler solution. https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/security/use-amazon-inspector-to-manage-your-build-and-deploy-pipelines-for-containerized-applications/

This approach integrates security scanning directly into the CI/CD pipeline and leverages AWS services for image scanning. Here's how it works: A new CodePipeline stage is added after the container image is built, but before it's pushed to Amazon ECR. ECR basic image scanning is configured to scan the image automatically upon push. This ensures that security scanning is part of the process. An AWS Lambda function is used as an action provider in the pipeline. This Lambda function can be configured to analyze the scan results of the image. If the Lambda function detects any security findings in the scan results, it can fail the pipeline, preventing the deployment of images with security vulnerabilities.

So it narrows down to option B and D which using ECR basic image scanning. B: create a stage D: add an action to the existing stage I'd go with D since executing an additional action will be faster than executing a whole stage.

The developer should choose option B. Create a new CodePipeline stage that occurs after the container image is built. Configure ECR basic image scanning to scan on image push. Use an AWS Lambda function as the action provider. Configure the Lambda function to check the scan results and to fail the pipeline if there are findings. This will allow the developer to place an analysis stage before deployment to analyze the container image earlier in the CI/CD pipeline with the most operational efficiency. CHATGPT
The question states "A developer needs to place an analysis stage" therefore I'd go with B.
Chat GPT says D

C) Eliminated - Scanning the source code directly does not analyze vulnerabilities specific to the container image D) Eliminated - Adding the scan at the deployment stage is too late in the pipeline. It defeats the purpose of early detection and could allow vulnerable images to proceed further in the pipeline before being flagged

B as per https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amplify/latest/userguide/running-tests.html You can run end-to-end (E2E) tests in the test phase of your Amplify app to catch regressions before pushing code to production. The test phase can be configured in the build specification YAML. Currently, you can run only the Cypress testing framework during a build. build specification is provided in the amplify.yml file
Amazon Q suggest D
B vs D: B: scan before pushing ECR (after the container image is built) D: scan after pushing ECR (before the deployment to the EKS cluster)
B is the correct answer.
ChatGPT says option B

question.en:
A developer is testing a new file storage application that uses an Amazon CloudFront distribution to serve content from an Amazon S3 bucket. The distribution accesses the S3 bucket by using an origin access identity (OAI). The S3 bucket's permissions explicitly deny access to all other users.
The application prompts users to authenticate on a login page and then uses signed cookies to allow users to access their personal storage directories. The developer has configured the distribution to use its default cache behavior with restricted viewer access and has set the origin to point to the S3 bucket. However, when the developer tries to navigate to the login page, the developer receives a 403 Forbidden error.
The developer needs to implement a solution to allow unauthenticated access to the login page. The solution also must keep all private content secure.
Which solution will meet these requirements?
question.ja:
あるデベロッパーが、Amazon S3 バケットからコンテンツを配信する Amazon CloudFront ディストリビューションを使用する新しいファイルストレージアプリケーションをテストしています。このディストリビューションは、origin access identity (OAI) を使用して S3 バケットにアクセスします。S3 バケットのパーミッションは、他のすべてのユーザーからのアクセスを明示的に拒否しています。
このアプリケーションは、ユーザーにログインページで認証を求め、その後 signed cookies を使用して、ユーザーが自分の個人用ストレージディレクトリにアクセスできるようにします。デベロッパーは、デフォルトのキャッシュビヘイビアを使用し、ビューアアクセスを制限するようにディストリビューションを設定し、オリジンを S3 バケットに向けて設定しています。しかし、デベロッパーがログインページに移動しようとすると、403 Forbidden エラーが発生します。
デベロッパーは、ログインページへの未認証アクセスを許可する解決策を実装する必要があります。この解決策は、すべてのプライベートコンテンツを安全に保つ必要もあります。
この要件を満たす解決策はどれですか。

text.en: Add a second cache behavior to the distribution with the same origin as the default cache behavior. Set the path pattern for the second cache behavior to the path of the login page, and make viewer access unrestricted. Keep the default cache behavior's settings unchanged.
text.ja: デフォルトのキャッシュビヘイビアと同じオリジンを持つ2つ目のキャッシュビヘイビアをディストリビューションに追加する。2つ目のキャッシュビヘイビアのパスパターンをログインページのパスに設定し、ビューアアクセスを無制限にする。デフォルトのキャッシュビヘイビアの設定は変更しない。

text.en: Add a second cache behavior to the distribution with the same origin as the default cache behavior. Set the path pattern for the second cache behavior to *, and make viewer access restricted. Change the default cache behavior's path pattern to the path of the login page, and make viewer access unrestricted.
text.ja: デフォルトのキャッシュビヘイビアと同じオリジンを持つ2つ目のキャッシュビヘイビアをディストリビューションに追加する。2つ目のキャッシュビヘイビアのパスパターンを * に設定し、ビューアアクセスを制限する。デフォルトのキャッシュビヘイビアのパスパターンをログインページのパスに変更し、ビューアアクセスを無制限にする。

text.en: Add a second origin as a failover origin to the default cache behavior. Point the failover origin to the S3 bucket. Set the path pattern for the primary origin to *, and make viewer access restricted. Set the path pattern for the failover origin to the path of the login page, and make viewer access unrestricted.
text.ja: デフォルトのキャッシュビヘイビアにフェイルオーバーオリジンとして2つ目のオリジンを追加する。フェイルオーバーオリジンを S3 バケットに向ける。プライマリオリジンのパスパターンを * に設定し、ビューアアクセスを制限する。フェイルオーバーオリジンのパスパターンをログインページのパスに設定し、ビューアアクセスを無制限にする。

text.en: Add a bucket policy to the S3 bucket to allow read access. Set the resource on the policy to the Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the login page object in the S3 bucket. Add a CloudFront function to the default cache behavior to redirect unauthorized requests to the login page's S3 URL.
text.ja: 読み取りアクセスを許可するバケットポリシーを S3 バケットに追加する。ポリシーのリソースを、S3 バケット内のログインページオブジェクトの Amazon Resource Name (ARN) に設定する。未承認のリクエストをログインページの S3 URL にリダイレクトする CloudFront function をデフォルトのキャッシュビヘイビアに追加する。

comment.en: A If you create additional cache behaviors, the default cache behavior is always the last to be processed. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/distribution-web-values-specify.html#DownloadDistValuesCacheBehavior
Should it be D? In case s3 bucket restricts permissions, those should be open for login.

By adding a second cache behavior with unrestricted viewer access to the login page's path pattern, unauthenticated users will be allowed to access the login page. At the same time, the default cache behavior's settings remain unchanged, and private content remains secure because it still requires signed cookies for access.

A) Adding a second cache behavior allows you to define specific rules for the login page while keeping the default settings for private content unchanged. B) Eliminated - Changing the default cache behavior to allow unrestricted access to the login page affects other private content, potentially compromising security. D) Eliminated - Adding a bucket policy to allow public access to the login page directly contradicts the requirement to use CloudFront for secure content delivery.

B) you cannot override the path pattern of the default Cache behavior C) the origin failover is used when the primary origin is not available, this is not our case D) with this configuration I think users wil get 403 Forbidden error and then redirected to the login page's S3 URL A is a workable approach in my opinion
A is the correct answer.

Answer is A. --The original way the developer had designed this application was too restrictive and didn't allow someone to even authenticate to get a signed cookie. By caching the second behavior, it allows the person authenticating to retrieve a cookie to access their personal data.
D cloud front function acts as lamda function

question.en:
A developer is using AWS Amplify Hosting to build and deploy an application. The developer is receiving an increased number of bug reports from users. The developer wants to add end-to-end testing to the application to eliminate as many bugs as possible before the bugs reach production.
Which solution should the developer implement to meet these requirements?
question.ja:
あるデベロッパーが、アプリケーションのビルドとデプロイに AWS Amplify Hosting を使用しています。このデベロッパーは、ユーザーからのバグ報告が増加していることに気づいています。デベロッパーは、できるだけ多くのバグが本番環境に到達する前に排除するために、アプリケーションに end-to-end テストを追加したいと考えています。
この要件を満たすために、デベロッパーはどの解決策を実装すべきですか。

text.en: Run the amplify add test command in the Amplify CLI.
text.ja: Amplify CLI で amplify add test コマンドを実行する。

text.en: Create unit tests in the application. Deploy the unit tests by using the amplify push command in the Amplify CLI.
text.ja: アプリケーションにユニットテストを作成する。Amplify CLI の amplify push コマンドを使用してユニットテストをデプロイする。

text.en: Add a test phase to the amplify.yml build settings for the application.
text.ja: アプリケーションの amplify.yml ビルド設定にテストフェーズを追加する。

text.en: Add a test phase to the aws-exports.js file for the application.
text.ja: アプリケーションの aws-exports.js ファイルにテストフェーズを追加する。

comment.en: Explanation: Adding a test phase to the amplify.yml build settings allows the developer to define and execute end-to-end tests as part of the build and deployment process in AWS Amplify Hosting. This will help ensure that bugs are caught and fixed before the application reaches production, improving the overall quality of the application.
C https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amplify/latest/userguide/running-tests.html
ton of thanks !! document commented 'End to End Test'
To implement end-to-end (E2E) testing before deployment, you can add a test phase to the amplify.yml file. A) Eliminated - Amplify CLI does not have a command named amplify add test.

A. Eliminado: Esse comando não existe na Amplify CLI. O Amplify não tem um comando direto para adicionar testes, então essa alternativa está errada. B. Eliminado: Testes unitários são importantes, mas eles não são testes de ponta a ponta (E2E). Além disso, amplify push serve para implantar mudanças na infraestrutura, não para rodar testes. D. Eliminado: O arquivo aws-exports.js contém configurações do Amplify para conectar o front-end aos serviços da AWS. Ele não é usado para definir fases de teste. Portanto, a melhor solução é a alternativa C, pois ela permite adicionar testes ao processo de build e evitar que bugs cheguem à produção.

A. Eliminado: Esse comando não existe na Amplify CLI. O Amplify não tem um comando direto para adicionar testes, então essa alternativa está errada. B. Eliminado: Testes unitários são importantes, mas eles não são testes de ponta a ponta (E2E). Além disso, amplify push serve para implantar mudanças na infraestrutura, não para rodar testes. D. Eliminado: O arquivo aws-exports.js contém configurações do Amplify para conectar o front-end aos serviços da AWS. Ele não é usado para definir fases de teste. Portanto, a melhor solução é a alternativa C, pois ela permite adicionar testes ao processo de build e evitar que bugs cheguem à produção.
This appear at 17 Jun exam
C is the correct answer.

B as per https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amplify/latest/userguide/running-tests.html You can run end-to-end (E2E) tests in the test phase of your Amplify app to catch regressions before pushing code to production. The test phase can be configured in the build specification YAML. Currently, you can run only the Cypress testing framework during a build. build specification is provided in the amplify.yml file
I'LL GO WITH B
We can use amplify.yml file to run any test commands at build time. Since the test must run while the program is being deployed (E2E) I'll go with B.

question.en:
An ecommerce company is using an AWS Lambda function behind Amazon API Gateway as its application tier. To process orders during checkout, the application calls a POST API from the frontend. The POST API invokes the Lambda function asynchronously. In rare situations, the application has not processed orders. The Lambda application logs show no errors or failures.
What should a developer do to solve this problem?
question.ja:
あるEコマース企業が、アプリケーション層として Amazon API Gateway の背後にある AWS Lambda 関数を使用しています。チェックアウト時の注文処理のために、アプリケーションはフロントエンドから POST API を呼び出します。この POST API は Lambda 関数を非同期に呼び出します。ごく稀に、アプリケーションが注文を処理しない場合があります。Lambda アプリケーションのログには、エラーや失敗は記録されていません。
この問題を解決するために、デベロッパーは何をすべきですか。

text.en: Inspect the frontend logs for API failures. Call the POST API manually by using the requests from the log file.
text.ja: フロントエンドのログで API の失敗を調査する。ログファイルのリクエストを使用して POST API を手動で呼び出す。

text.en: Create and inspect the Lambda dead-letter queue. Troubleshoot the failed functions. Reprocess the events.
text.ja: Lambda のデッドレターキューを作成して調査する。失敗した関数をトラブルシューティングする。イベントを再処理する。

text.en: Inspect the Lambda logs in Amazon CloudWatch for possible errors. Fix the errors.
text.ja: Amazon CloudWatch で Lambda のログを調査し、考えられるエラーを確認する。エラーを修正する。

text.en: Make sure that caching is disabled for the POST API in API Gateway.
text.ja: API Gateway で POST API のキャッシュが無効になっていることを確認する。

comment.en: Explanation: By configuring a dead-letter queue (DLQ) for the Lambda function, you can capture asynchronous invocation events that were not successfully processed. This allows you to troubleshoot the failed functions and reprocess the events, ensuring that orders are not missed. The DLQ will hold information about the failed events, allowing you to analyze and resolve the issue.

A The Lambda function might have not been called since the Lambda logs show no errors or failures. The cause might be that the frontend application does not call the API or an error occurs in the API Gateway processing.
as you said "... events that were not successfully processed." but there is not failure in Lambda log, so the lambda was not invoked by the POST API event. B is id not the answer.

Asynchronous invocation means that the caller of the lambda does not wait for a response. The type of invocation has no effect on the lambda having logs or not. I picked A, because the lambda not having logs suggests something’s gone wrong upstream of the lambda.

since there are no logs of errors or failures, the issue is likely due to dropped asynchronous events. Configuring a DLQ will capture these dropped events for further analysis and reprocessing. A) Eliminated - While inspecting frontend logs can help diagnose client-side issues, the problem here involves unprocessed events in Lambda. C) Eliminated - The question states that there are no errors or failures in the Lambda logs D) Eliminated - API Gateway caching is not related to this issue. Even with caching enabled, events would still invoke the Lambda function.
I don't like B which has reprocess the errors, which will make a whole load of errors be process creating orders which could be months old

I think D should be the correct answer to this question. The logs have no indications of errors or failed events, so if some transactions are not being processed, that probably means that the lambda function wasn't invoked for those calls. One reason could be that caching is enabled in API gateway for the POST request, so the lambda function isn't triggered for any cache hits. - A is not correct as the frontend would be getting 202s for all asynchronous post requests. - B is not correct because lambda logs have no errors => no lambda execution errors => DLQ won't get any requests of interest if we enable it. A comment below mentioned that asynchronous lambda invocations don't generate logs, but that is not true. - C is obviously incorrect. The premise explicitly mentions that there aren't any errors in the logs.
Its an asynchronous invocation events, that's y there is no log. Because in asynchronous its not mandatory to get the result after invocation events.
Read it carefully: "The Lambda application logs show no errors or failures" There are logs, so the lambda was called answer B

Option C is the appropriate choice because it involves inspecting the Lambda logs in Amazon CloudWatch to identify any potential issues or errors that might be causing the orders not to be processed Option B is not the most appropriate choice because the dead-letter queue is generally used to capture events that cannot be processed by a Lambda function. In this scenario, it seems that the Lambda function is executing without apparent errors. Thus, the issue might not be related to dead-letter queue failures.
Caching is only for GET Requests not for POST Requests. Correct answer is B

A.Eliminada: O problema ocorre no backend (Lambda), e não no frontend. Como a invocação é assíncrona, o frontend não tem controle sobre falhas no processamento. C. Eliminada: Já foi mencionado que os logs do Lambda não mostram erros ou falhas, então apenas inspecionar os logs do CloudWatch não ajudaria a encontrar a causa do problema. D. Eliminada: O cache no API Gateway não afeta requisições POST, pois o cache só é aplicado para métodos GET. Então essa alternativa não faz sentido.

question.en:
A company is building a web application on AWS. When a customer sends a request, the application will generate reports and then make the reports available to the customer within one hour. Reports should be accessible to the customer for 8 hours. Some reports are larger than 1 MB. Each report is unique to the customer. The application should delete all reports that are older than 2 days.
Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
question.ja:
ある企業がAWS上でWebアプリケーションを構築している。顧客がリクエストを送信すると、アプリケーションはレポートを生成し、1時間以内に顧客がそのレポートを利用できるようにする。レポートは顧客が8時間アクセスできる必要がある。レポートの中には1MBを超えるものもある。各レポートは顧客ごとに一意である。アプリケーションは2日より古いすべてのレポートを削除する必要がある。
これらの要件を最小の運用オーバーヘッドで満たす解決策はどれか。

text.en: Generate the reports and then store the reports as Amazon DynamoDB items that have a specified TTL. Generate a URL that retrieves the reports from DynamoDB. Provide the URL to customers through the web application.
text.ja: レポートを生成し、TTLを指定したAmazon DynamoDBアイテムとして保存する。DynamoDBからレポートを取得するURLを生成する。そのURLをWebアプリケーション経由で顧客に提供する。

text.en: Generate the reports and then store the reports in an Amazon S3 bucket that uses server-side encryption. Attach the reports to an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) message. Subscribe the customer to email notifications from Amazon SNS.
text.ja: レポートを生成し、サーバーサイド暗号化を使用するAmazon S3バケットに保存する。レポートをAmazon Simple Notification Service（Amazon SNS）メッセージに添付する。顧客をAmazon SNSのメール通知に登録する。

text.en: Generate the reports and then store the reports in an Amazon S3 bucket that uses server-side encryption. Generate a presigned URL that contains an expiration date Provide the URL to customers through the web application. Add S3 Lifecycle configuration rules to the S3 bucket to delete old reports.
text.ja: レポートを生成し、サーバーサイド暗号化を使用するAmazon S3バケットに保存する。有効期限を含むpresigned URLを生成する。そのURLをWebアプリケーション経由で顧客に提供する。S3バケットにS3ライフサイクル設定ルールを追加して古いレポートを削除する。

text.en: Generate the reports and then store the reports in an Amazon RDS database with a date stamp. Generate an URL that retrieves the reports from the RDS database. Provide the URL to customers through the web application. Schedule an hourly AWS Lambda function to delete database records that have expired date stamps.
text.ja: レポートを生成し、日付スタンプ付きでAmazon RDSデータベースに保存する。RDSデータベースからレポートを取得するURLを生成する。そのURLをWebアプリケーション経由で顧客に提供する。期限切れの日付スタンプを持つデータベースレコードを削除するAWS Lambda関数を1時間ごとに実行するようスケジュールする。

comment.en: Explanation: Storing the reports in an Amazon S3 bucket provides a cost-effective and scalable solution for handling files larger than 1 MB. Server-side encryption ensures data security. Generating a presigned URL with an expiration date allows the customer to access the report for 8 hours, and S3 Lifecycle configuration rules automatically delete the reports older than 2 days, reducing operational overhead.
Presigned URL

A) DynamoDB cannot store object larger than 400K B) SNS cannot send email with attachment - https://repost.aws/questions/QUOvaKJVb3QzOqVENONBZUag/sns-send-file-attachment D) the nature or format of the report is not specified, however RDS doent look like a great place to store large document file. Also generating a url to the reports from the RDS database requires some work while it is a native capabilities in S3 C) is a workable solution as S3 is designed to store file objects, it allows to easily generate pre-signed url, and provide lifecycle management rule that allows to expire objects

Dynamo DB cannot store object > 400KB -> option A is out immediately. Limited access to S3 calls for presigned URL which is option C. C also has lifecycle config to delete old object while B does not have that. D is possible but too much effort compared to design pattern in C.
C https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/using-presigned-url.html https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/object-lifecycle-mgmt.html
The 1MB condition denies the TTL option so C is best

S3 Lifecycle rules can automatically delete objects after 2 days, reducing operational overhead by eliminating the need for custom cleanup logic A) Eliminated - DynamoDB is not designed to store large objects (e.g., reports larger than 1 MB). D) Eliminated - Storing large reports in a relational database like RDS is inefficient and costly compared to S3.
B) Eliminated - Attaching reports to SNS messages is impractical because SNS is not designed for large file attachments.
C is the correct answer.
C presigned and lifecycle rules to move

question.en:
A company has deployed an application on AWS Elastic Beanstalk. The company has configured the Auto Scaling group that is associated with the Elastic Beanstalk environment to have five Amazon EC2 instances. If the capacity is fewer than four EC2 instances during the deployment, application performance degrades. The company is using the all-at-once deployment policy.
What is the MOST cost-effective way to solve the deployment issue?
question.ja:
ある企業がAWS Elastic Beanstalk上にアプリケーションをデプロイしている。この企業は、Elastic Beanstalk環境に関連付けられたAuto Scalingグループを5台のAmazon EC2インスタンスで構成している。デプロイ中にキャパシティが4台未満のEC2インスタンスになると、アプリケーションのパフォーマンスが低下する。この企業はall-at-onceデプロイポリシーを使用している。
このデプロイの問題を解決する最もコスト効率の良い方法はどれか。

text.en: Change the Auto Scaling group to six desired instances.
text.ja: Auto Scalingグループの希望インスタンス数を6台に変更する。

text.en: Change the deployment policy to traffic splitting. Specify an evaluation time of 1 hour.
text.ja: デプロイポリシーをtraffic splittingに変更する。評価時間を1時間に指定する。

text.en: Change the deployment policy to rolling with additional batch. Specify a batch size of 1.
text.ja: デプロイポリシーをrolling with additional batchに変更する。バッチサイズを1に指定する。

text.en: Change the deployment policy to rolling. Specify a batch size of 2.
text.ja: デプロイポリシーをrollingに変更する。バッチサイズを2に指定する。

comment.en: Explanation: The rolling with additional batch deployment policy allows Elastic Beanstalk to launch additional instances in a new batch before terminating the old instances. In this case, specifying a batch size of 1 means that Elastic Beanstalk will deploy the application updates to 1 new instance at a time, ensuring that there are always at least 4 instances available during the deployment process. This method maintains application performance while minimizing the additional cost.

1. Rolling with additional batch deployment: This type of deployment maintains full capacity while new application versions are deployed. It launches a new batch of instances with the new application version, and if the new batch is healthy, it terminates a batch of instances running the old application version. 2. Batch size of 1: This will ensure that one new instance is launched with the new version of the application. Once it is deemed healthy, one of the old instances will be terminated. This will continue until all instances are running the new version, ensuring the capacity is never less than four instances. This approach will add only a minimal additional cost for the temporary overlapping instances during deployment.
C https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticbeanstalk/latest/dg/using-features.rolling-version-deploy.html

A) Eliminated - Permanently increasing the desired capacity to six EC2 instances incurs unnecessary ongoing costs. B) Eliminated - Traffic splitting is primarily used for canary testing or gradual traffic shifting. It does not guarantee maintaining the desired instance capacity during deployment. D) Eliminated - A rolling deployment with a batch size of 2 means two instances are replaced at a time. With a fixed capacity of 5 instances, this would temporarily reduce the number of running instances to 3, causing performance degradation.

Option D is the best solution because it allows the company to update the application without losing service or reducing availability significantly, and without increasing the cost or complexity of the solution.
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticbeanstalk/latest/dg/using-features.rolling-version-deploy.html

The correct answer is: D. Change the deployment policy to rolling. Specify a batch size of 2. A rolling deployment policy will deploy the new application version to one batch of instances at a time, while the other batches continue to serve traffic. This ensures that the application always has at least four instances available during the deployment. Specifying a batch size of 2 means that two instances will be deployed at a time. This is the most cost-effective option because it minimizes the number of instances that are needed to maintain application performance during the deployment. The other options are not as cost-effective because they require more instances to be running during the deployment. Option A requires six instances, option B requires at least five instances, and option C requires at least four instances.

The rolling deployment policy updates a few instances at a time, but unlike the "rolling with additional batch" option, it does not launch new instances before terminating the old ones. Therefore, capacity could drop below four during deployment, affecting application performance.

If batch size of 1: During the time the new instances are being deployed and are not yet in service, there are only 5 - 2 = 3 old instances available to serve the traffic, which violates the requirement to maintain at least 4 instances to avoid performance degradation. so, i go with A answer.

C) Batch Size of 1: Specifies that only one instance will be updated at a time, minimizing downtime and maintaining the required instance capacity for performance. Rolling with additional Batch - This deployment strategy adds an extra batch of EC2 instances during deployment. After updating one batch, the additional instances are terminated, ensuring cost efficiency.
C is the correct answer.
the requirement is not to go below 4 instances. Option D specifies a batch size of 2 which would lead to 3 running instances. The correct option is C

question.en:
A developer is incorporating AWS X-Ray into an application that handles personal identifiable information (PII). The application is hosted on Amazon EC2 instances. The application trace messages include encrypted PII and go to Amazon CloudWatch. The developer needs to ensure that no PII goes outside of the EC2 instances.
Which solution will meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある開発者が、個人を特定できる情報（PII）を扱うアプリケーションにAWS X-Rayを組み込んでいる。このアプリケーションはAmazon EC2インスタンス上でホストされている。アプリケーションのトレースメッセージには暗号化されたPIIが含まれ、Amazon CloudWatchに送信される。開発者は、PIIがEC2インスタンスの外部に出ないようにする必要がある。
この要件を満たす解決策はどれか。

text.en: Manually instrument the X-Ray SDK in the application code.
text.ja: アプリケーションコードでX-Ray SDKを手動でインスツルメンテーションする。

text.en: Use the X-Ray auto-instrumentation agent.
text.ja: X-Rayの自動インスツルメンテーションエージェントを使用する。

text.en: Use Amazon Macie to detect and hide PII. Call the X-Ray API from AWS Lambda.
text.ja: Amazon MacieでPIIを検出して非表示にする。AWS LambdaからX-Ray APIを呼び出す。

text.en: Use AWS Distro for Open Telemetry.
text.ja: AWS Distro for OpenTelemetryを使用する。

comment.en: Explanation: By manually instrumenting the X-Ray SDK in the application code, the developer can have full control over which data is included in the trace messages. This way, the developer can ensure that no PII is sent to X-Ray by carefully handling the PII within the application and not including it in the trace messages.

A Not to send any PII to AWS X-Ray service, add instrumentation code in your application at each location to send trace information that PII is eliminated. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/xray/latest/devguide/xray-instrumenting-your-app.html
Option B, using the X-Ray auto-instrumentation agent, is the most appropriate solution for ensuring that no PII goes outside of the EC2 instances.
C is wrong, Amazon Macie discover PII but dont hide it

X-Ray auto-instrumentation agent itself does not inherently remove or redact Personally Identifiable Information (PII). The primary purpose of the auto-instrumentation agent is to automate the process of instrumenting supported frameworks and libraries for tracing with AWS X-Ray. When dealing with PII or any sensitive information, the responsibility for ensuring that such data is not exposed in traces lies with the application code and configuration, rather than the X-Ray auto-instrumentation agent. While the X-Ray auto-instrumentation agent simplifies the instrumentation process, the need for precise control over PII and the ability to implement custom security measures make manual instrumentation more suitable in this scenario.

The X-Ray auto-instrumentation agent can help ensure that sensitive information like PII is not transmitted outside of the EC2 instances. It automatically instruments the application without requiring manual intervention, making it easier to maintain traceability without risking the exposure of sensitive data. Options A and D involve manual or custom instrumentations, which might inadvertently expose PII if not implemented correctly. Option C, using Amazon Macie to detect and hide PII and calling the X-Ray API from Lambda, might add complexity to the architecture and doesn't directly address the prevention of PII leaving the EC2 instances.

The X-Ray auto-instrumentation agent is designed to automatically trace and collect data from AWS resources and services without requiring manual instrumentation in your application code. It helps ensure that sensitive information, such as PII, remains within the EC2 instances by not transmitting the data outside explicitly. The agent focuses on tracing the application behavior and performance without directly sending PII to external services. This solution is suitable for ensuring compliance and data security while still benefiting from X-Ray's tracing and insights.
Option "B" : Because. Avoids human error.

Using the X-Ray auto-instrumentation agent (Option B) is the best choice in this scenario because it will automatically instrument the application without requiring any manual code changes. Additionally, when using X-Ray with auto-instrumentation, you can control the sampling rate to ensure that only a subset of trace data (and encrypted PII) is sent to X-Ray and CloudWatch, reducing the risk of sensitive data being exposed outside of the instances.
For non-Java applications running on EC2 instances, you will need to use the appropriate X-Ray SDKs to manually instrument the application code. You can't use auto-agent

Currently, with the all-at-once deployment policy, Elastic Beanstalk updates all EC2 instances simultaneously. This can cause issues with application performance, as the entire fleet of instances is replaced at once, leading to potential downtime or degraded performance if the number of available instances falls below the necessary threshold (fewer than four EC2 instances in your case). Switching to a rolling deployment with a batch size of 2 will allow Elastic Beanstalk to update the instances in smaller batches. During each batch update, only two instances will be updated at a time, which means that there will always be a sufficient number of EC2 instances running (at least three instances at all times). This avoids the performance degradation caused by having fewer than four EC2 instances available.
Wrong question

question.en:
A developer is migrating some features from a legacy monolithic application to use AWS Lambda functions instead. The application currently stores data in an Amazon Aurora DB cluster that runs in private subnets in a VPC. The AWS account has one VPC deployed. The Lambda functions and the DB cluster are deployed in the same AWS Region in the same AWS account.
The developer needs to ensure that the Lambda functions can securely access the DB cluster without crossing the public internet.
Which solution will meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある開発者が、レガシーなモノリシックアプリケーションの一部の機能をAWS Lambda関数を使用する形式に移行している。アプリケーションは現在、VPC内のプライベートサブネットで実行されているAmazon Aurora DBクラスターにデータを保存している。このAWSアカウントには1つのVPCがデプロイされている。Lambda関数とDBクラスターは、同じAWSリージョンの同じAWSアカウント内にデプロイされている。
開発者は、Lambda関数がパブリックインターネットを経由せずにDBクラスターに安全にアクセスできるようにする必要がある。
この要件を満たす解決策はどれか。

text.en: Configure the DB cluster's public access setting to Yes.
text.ja: DBクラスターのパブリックアクセス設定を「Yes」に構成する。

text.en: Configure an Amazon RDS database proxy for he Lambda functions.
text.ja: Lambda関数用にAmazon RDSデータベースプロキシを構成する。

text.en: Configure a NAT gateway and a security group for the Lambda functions.
text.ja: Lambda関数用にNATゲートウェイとセキュリティグループを構成する。

text.en: Configure the VPC, subnets, and a security group for the Lambda functions.
text.ja: Lambda関数用にVPC、サブネット、セキュリティグループを構成する。

comment.en: Option D is the right answer. When we want the lambda to privately access the DB cluster instead of moving the traffic over the public internet, we need to have the lambda and db cluster to be in the same VPC. When we configure the VPC, subnets, and a security group for the lambda function, the lambda function will be able to communicate with the db cluster using the private IPs that are associated to the VPC. NAT gateway comes into use when you have the lambda deployed in a private subnet and you would want to provide internet access to it.

Explanation: To securely access the Amazon Aurora DB cluster without crossing the public internet, the Lambda functions need to be configured to run within the same VPC as the DB cluster. This involves configuring the VPC, subnets, and a security group for the Lambda functions. This setup ensures that the Lambda functions can communicate with the DB cluster using private IP addresses within the VPC.
D is correct, NATGateway is for when we want Lambda to access the public when it is in a private VPC
D https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/foundation-networking.html
B https://repost.aws/questions/QULXSqEPGbQx6qiyBa1D1Udg/lambda-to-db-connectivity-best-practices
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-vpc.html

Lambda functions can be configured to run within a VPC. By assigning the Lambda functions to the same VPC and private subnets as the Aurora DB cluster, the communication remains internal to the VPC and does not cross the public internet. Configuring the security group ensures that the Lambda functions can securely connect to the Aurora DB cluster by allowing appropriate inbound/outbound rules.
Ans is D.
D is the correct answer.
Actually Proxy should be on the same VPC as the database and since lambda is in another vpc it doesnt have access unless a connection happens between these two vpc or just option D

Selected Answer: B https://www.udemy.com/course/aws-certified-developer-associate-dva-c01/learn/lecture/36527788#overview https://aws.amazon.com/ru/blogs/compute/using-amazon-rds-proxy-with-aws-lambda/
After reading doc in the link that you mention, my conclusion is D NAT GW is required if Lamba in the user VPC need to access internet

question.en:
A developer is building a new application on AWS. The application uses an AWS Lambda function that retrieves information from an Amazon DynamoDB table. The developer hard coded the DynamoDB table name into the Lambda function code. The table name might change over time. The developer does not want to modify the Lambda code if the table name changes.
Which solution will meet these requirements MOST efficiently?
question.ja:
ある開発者が、AWS上で新しいアプリケーションを構築している。このアプリケーションは、Amazon DynamoDBテーブルから情報を取得するAWS Lambda関数を使用している。開発者はDynamoDBのテーブル名をLambda関数のコードにハードコーディングした。テーブル名は今後変更される可能性がある。開発者は、テーブル名が変更された場合にLambdaコードを変更したくない。
この要件を最も効率的に満たす解決策はどれか。

text.en: Create a Lambda environment variable to store the table name. Use the standard method for the programming language to retrieve the variable.
text.ja: テーブル名を格納するLambda環境変数を作成する。プログラミング言語の標準的な方法でその変数を取得する。

text.en: Store the table name in a file. Store the file in the /tmp folder. Use the SDK for the programming language to retrieve the table name.
text.ja: テーブル名をファイルに保存する。そのファイルを/tmpフォルダに保存する。プログラミング言語のSDKを使用してテーブル名を取得する。

text.en: Create a file to store the table name. Zip the file and upload the file to the Lambda layer. Use the SDK for the programming language to retrieve the table name.
text.ja: テーブル名を保存するファイルを作成する。そのファイルをzipしてLambdaレイヤーにアップロードする。プログラミング言語のSDKを使用してテーブル名を取得する。

text.en: Create a global variable that is outside the handler in the Lambda function to store the table name.
text.ja: Lambda関数のハンドラーの外側にグローバル変数を作成してテーブル名を保存する。

comment.en: You need to use environment variables
A https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-envvars.html

Explanation: Using Lambda environment variables allows you to store configuration information separate from your code, which makes it easy to update the table name without changing the Lambda function code. AWS Lambda provides built-in support for environment variables, making it the most efficient solution.

You can use environment variables to adjust your function's behavior without updating code. An environment variable is a pair of strings that is stored in a function's version-specific configuration. The Lambda runtime makes environment variables available to your code and sets additional environment variables that contain information about the function and invocation request.
Environment variables are a built-in feature of AWS Lambda
A is the correct answer.
Why are some answers wrong on here?

question.en:
A company has a critical application on AWS. The application exposes an HTTP API by using Amazon API Gateway. The API is integrated with an AWS Lambda function. The application stores data in an Amazon RDS for MySQL DB instance with 2 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) and 64 GB of RAM.

Customers have reported that some of the API calls return HTTP 500 Internal Server Error responses. Amazon CloudWatch Logs shows errors for “too many connections.” The errors occur during peak usage times that are unpredictable.

The company needs to make the application resilient. The database cannot be down outside of scheduled maintenance hours.

Which solution will meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある企業がAWS上で重要なアプリケーションを運用している。このアプリケーションはAmazon API Gatewayを使用してHTTP APIを公開している。このAPIはAWS Lambda関数と統合されている。アプリケーションは2 vCPUと64GBのRAMを持つAmazon RDS for MySQL DBインスタンスにデータを保存している。

顧客から、一部のAPI呼び出しがHTTP 500 Internal Server Errorレスポンスを返すという報告があった。Amazon CloudWatch Logsには「too many connections」というエラーが表示されている。このエラーは、予測できないピーク利用時間帯に発生している。

この企業は、アプリケーションの回復性を高める必要がある。データベースは、予定されたメンテナンス時間以外にダウンしてはならない。

これらの要件を満たす解決策はどれか。

text.en: Decrease the number of vCPUs for the DB instance. Increase the max_connections setting.
text.ja: DBインスタンスのvCPU数を減らす。max_connections設定を増やす。

text.en: Use Amazon RDS Proxy to create a proxy that connects to the DB instance. Update the Lambda function to connect to the proxy.
text.ja: Amazon RDS Proxyを使用して、DBインスタンスに接続するプロキシを作成する。Lambda関数をそのプロキシに接続するように更新する。

text.en: Add a CloudWatch alarm that changes the DB instance class when the number of connections increases to more than 1,000.
text.ja: 接続数が1,000を超えたときにDBインスタンスクラスを変更するCloudWatchアラームを追加する。

text.en: Add an Amazon EventBridge rule that increases the max_connections setting of the DB instance when CPU utilization is above 75%.
text.ja: CPU使用率が75%を超えたときにDBインスタンスのmax_connections設定を増やすAmazon EventBridgeルールを追加する。

comment.en: The best solution to meet these requirements would be to use Amazon RDS Proxy to create a proxy that connects to the DB instance and update the Lambda function to connect to the proxy.

A) Eliminated - Decreasing the number of vCPUs will worsen performance. B) When your Lambda function wants to query the database, instead of opening a new connection every time (which is what causes "too many connections"), it talks to the RDS Proxy. For example, if 100 Lambda functions try to connect at the same time, instead of opening 100 database connections, the RDS Proxy might only open 10 and reuse them. C) Eliminated - It incurs unnecessary costs for increased capacity, even if the issue is caused by connection management.

Amazon RDS Proxy is designed to handle a large number of simultaneous connections efficiently. It sits between your application and your RDS database to pool and share database connections, improving database efficiency and application scalability. This approach can reduce the number of connections to the database and handle unpredictable peak loads more effectively.
It’s B. RDS proxy can handle many open connections to the database.

Adding an Amazon EventBridge rule to increase the max_connections setting based on CPU utilization is not directly addressing the issue of too many connections. Additionally, focusing solely on CPU utilization might not be the best metric for handling connection-related issues.
B is the correct answer.

B: RDS Proxy establishes and manages the necessary connection pools to your database so that your Lambda function creates fewer database connections¹. RDS Proxy also handles failovers and retries automatically, which improves the availability of your application. A will reduce the performance and capacity of the database. C may incur additional charges for scaling up the DB instance. It may also cause downtime during the scaling process, which violates the requirement that the database cannot be down outside of scheduled maintenance hours. D may not react fast enough to handle unpredictable peak usage times. It may also cause memory issues if the max_connections setting is too high.
I think D is incorrect because it increases the number of connections based on the CPU consumption not the number of connections.
https://repost.aws/knowledge-center/rds-mysql-max-connections
There should not be any downtime. Create an Event bridge rule to update the max_connections parameter in Parameter group of DB instance.

question.en:
A company has installed smart meters in all its customer locations. The smart meters measure power usage at 1-minute intervals and send the usage readings to a remote endpoint for collection. The company needs to create an endpoint that will receive the smart meter readings and store the readings in a database. The company wants to store the location ID and timestamp information.

The company wants to give its customers low-latency access to their current usage and historical usage on demand. The company expects demand to increase significantly. The solution must not impact performance or include downtime while scaling.

Which solution will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?
question.ja:
ある企業が、すべての顧客の設置場所にスマートメーターを導入している。スマートメーターは1分間隔で電力使用量を計測し、その使用量データを収集用のリモートエンドポイントへ送信する。この企業は、スマートメーターの計測値を受信してデータベースに保存するエンドポイントを作成する必要がある。企業は、位置ID（location ID）とタイムスタンプの情報を保存したいと考えている。

この企業は、顧客に現在の使用量と過去の使用量へのオンデマンドかつ低レイテンシーのアクセスを提供したいと考えている。企業は需要が大幅に増加すると予想している。このソリューションは、スケーリング中にパフォーマンスへの影響やダウンタイムを発生させてはならない。

これらの要件を最もコスト効率良く満たす解決策はどれか。

text.en: Store the smart meter readings in an Amazon RDS database. Create an index on the location ID and timestamp columns. Use the columns to filter on the customers' data.
text.ja: スマートメーターの計測値をAmazon RDSデータベースに保存する。location IDとタイムスタンプの列にインデックスを作成する。これらの列を使用して顧客のデータをフィルタリングする。

text.en: Store the smart meter readings in an Amazon DynamoDB table. Create a composite key by using the location ID and timestamp columns. Use the columns to filter on the customers' data.
text.ja: スマートメーターの計測値をAmazon DynamoDBテーブルに保存する。location IDとタイムスタンプの列を使用して複合キーを作成する。これらの列を使用して顧客のデータをフィルタリングする。

text.en: Store the smart meter readings in Amazon ElastiCache for Redis. Create a SortedSet key by using the location ID and timestamp columns. Use the columns to filter on the customers' data.
text.ja: スマートメーターの計測値をAmazon ElastiCache for Redisに保存する。location IDとタイムスタンプの列を使用してSortedSetキーを作成する。これらの列を使用して顧客のデータをフィルタリングする。

text.en: Store the smart meter readings in Amazon S3. Partition the data by using the location ID and timestamp columns. Use Amazon Athena to filter on the customers' data.
text.ja: スマートメーターの計測値をAmazon S3に保存する。location IDとタイムスタンプの列を使用してデータをパーティション分割する。Amazon Athenaを使用して顧客のデータをフィルタリングする。

comment.en: The most cost-effective solution to meet these requirements would be to store the smart meter readings in an Amazon DynamoDB table and create a composite key using the location ID and timestamp columns
Going with B. DynamoDB is the most cost-effective solution.

This solution provides low-latency access to real-time and historical data, scales seamlessly to accommodate increased demand without downtime, and is likely to be more cost-effective than the alternatives for this specific use case. DynamoDB's managed service nature also reduces the administrative burden of managing the database.
C is the right answer
You need to use Athena as well to do partitoning
I miss you Ruchi

DynamoDB is purpose-built for low-latency, scalable storage of high-frequency, time-series data. Composite key design (location ID + timestamp) enables efficient querying. Automatically scales without downtime or performance impact.
B is the correct answer.
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/bp-sort-keys.html

question.en:
A company is building a serverless application that uses AWS Lambda functions. The company needs to create a set of test events to test Lambda functions in a development environment. The test events will be created once and then will be used by all the developers in an IAM developer group. The test events must be editable by any of the IAM users in the IAM developer group.

Which solution will meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある企業が、AWS Lambda関数を使用するサーバーレスアプリケーションを構築している。この企業は、開発環境でLambda関数をテストするためのテストイベントのセットを作成する必要がある。テストイベントは一度だけ作成され、IAM開発者グループ内のすべての開発者によって使用される。テストイベントは、そのIAM開発者グループ内の任意のIAMユーザーが編集できる必要がある。

これらの要件を満たす解決策はどれか。

text.en: Create and store the test events in Amazon S3 as JSON objects. Allow S3 bucket access to all IAM users.
text.ja: テストイベントを作成し、Amazon S3にJSONオブジェクトとして保存する。すべてのIAMユーザーにS3バケットへのアクセスを許可する。

text.en: Create the test events. Configure the event sharing settings to make the test events shareable.
text.ja: テストイベントを作成する。テストイベントを共有可能にするようにイベント共有設定を構成する。

text.en: Create and store the test events in Amazon DynamoDB. Allow access to DynamoDB by using IAM roles.
text.ja: テストイベントを作成し、Amazon DynamoDBに保存する。IAMロールを使用してDynamoDBへのアクセスを許可する。

text.en: Create the test events. Configure the event sharing settings to make the test events private.
text.ja: テストイベントを作成する。テストイベントを非公開にするようにイベント共有設定を構成する。

comment.en: Under the "Test" tab there's an option. (Shareable) This event is available to IAM users within the same account who have permissions to access and use shareable events. You can check this by yourself on the Lambda Also, here's a documentation https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/testing-functions.html#creating-shareable-events
Since March of this year, this is now possible to share test events within the same account with different users.
I meant to select A
(Shareable events) directly leverages AWS Lambda's built-in functionality, reducing the need for custom storage and retrieval mechanisms.
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/testing-functions.html
B is the correct answer.

This option is the most straightforward and aligns with AWS practices for managing shared resources like test events. IAM policies can be configured to grant the necessary permissions to the developer group, ensuring that all members can access and edit the test events stored in S3. This method leverages the scalability and security features of S3, along with the granular permission control provided by IAM, to meet the requirements.
Answer: B https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2022/03/aws-lambda-console-test-events/

This option is viable. Amazon S3 can store JSON objects (test events), and access to these objects can be controlled through S3 bucket policies or IAM policies. By setting the correct permissions, all IAM users in the developer group can read and write to the S3 bucket, enabling them to edit and use the test events.
Selected Answer: A The key Concept here is Sharing - test events in the Lambda console are for individual account can't be used by other developers

This approach ensures that the test events are stored centrally in an S3 bucket where all IAM users within the developer group have access. By granting access to the S3 bucket to all IAM users, any user within the group can create, edit, and retrieve the test events, meeting the requirement for collaborative access and editing. Options B and D don't directly address the need for IAM users to edit the test events; sharing settings might allow access, but they might not allow editing by all IAM users in the group. Option C, using DynamoDB, requires specific IAM role configurations for each user, which could become complex to manage and might not provide the same level of straightforward access and editing capability for all users within the IAM group.
Based on ChatGPT:A

question.en:
A developer is configuring an application's deployment environment in AWS CodePipeline. The application code is stored in a GitHub repository. The developer wants to ensure that the repository package's unit tests run in the new deployment environment. The developer has already set the pipeline's source provider to GitHub and has specified the repository and branch to use in the deployment.

Which combination of steps should the developer take next to meet these requirements with the LEAST overhead? (Choose two.)
question.ja:
ある開発者が、AWS CodePipelineでアプリケーションのデプロイ環境を構成している。アプリケーションコードはGitHubリポジトリに保存されている。開発者は、新しいデプロイ環境でリポジトリパッケージのユニットテストが実行されることを確認したいと考えている。開発者は、パイプラインのソースプロバイダーをGitHubに設定し、デプロイに使用するリポジトリとブランチを既に指定している。

これらの要件を最小のオーバーヘッドで満たすために、開発者が次に実行すべき手順の組み合わせはどれか。（2つ選択）

text.en: Create an AWS CodeCommit project. Add the repository package's build and test commands to the project's buildspec.
text.ja: AWS CodeCommitプロジェクトを作成する。プロジェクトのbuildspecにリポジトリパッケージのビルドとテストのコマンドを追加する。

text.en: Create an AWS CodeBuild project. Add the repository package's build and test commands to the project's buildspec.
text.ja: AWS CodeBuildプロジェクトを作成する。プロジェクトのbuildspecにリポジトリパッケージのビルドとテストのコマンドを追加する。

text.en: Create an AWS CodeDeploy project. Add the repository package's build and test commands to the project's buildspec.
text.ja: AWS CodeDeployプロジェクトを作成する。プロジェクトのbuildspecにリポジトリパッケージのビルドとテストのコマンドを追加する。
