text.ja: 注文 ID を Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) トピックに送信する。在庫管理用と決済処理用の Lambda 関数をそのトピックにサブスクライブする。

text.en: Deliver the order ID to an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. Configure the Lambda functions for inventory management and payment processing to poll the queue.
text.ja: 注文 ID を Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) キューに配信する。在庫管理用と決済処理用の Lambda 関数がそのキューをポーリングするように設定する。

comment.en: A. The only viable solution

Here's why this solution is most appropriate: SNS FIFO Topic: A First-In-First-Out (FIFO) SNS topic ensures that messages are delivered in the exact order they are sent. This is critical for maintaining the order of ticket requests. SQS FIFO Queues: By having two separate FIFO queues for inventory management and payment processing, the application can process these aspects in parallel while still maintaining the order integrity. The FIFO nature of the queues ensures that if a seat is sold more than once, the first order received is processed first. Order Processing Logic: With this setup, if the first order is rejected during payment processing, the second order can be processed next. The sequential processing inherent in FIFO queues ensures that this logic can be correctly implemented.

None of these answers are correct. People say it is A - well then what prevents payment processing for order #1 if the inventory lambda failed to allocate a seat for order #1? The correct answer should be for the order processing lambda to place orders into a queue (e.g. FIFO SQS) and then seat allocation lambda should poll this queue, check if seating is available and if so it should then invoke the payment processing lambda. This option isn't available. The next best option would be Option B. Better than A, since you won't be charged for an order where seating isn't available. If I am wrong, please explain it. Would love to hear. Thanks
FIFO is the only option that maintains ordering.
D. Deliver the order ID to an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. Configure the Lambda functions for inventory management and payment processing to poll the queue.
A is the correct answer.
D. Deliver the order ID to an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. Configure the Lambda functions for inventory management and payment processing to poll the queue.

question.en:
An application uses AWS X-Ray to generate a large amount of trace data on an hourly basis. A developer wants to use filter expressions to limit the returned results through user-specified custom attributes.

How should the developer use filter expressions to filter the results in X-Ray?
question.ja:
あるアプリケーションは、AWS X-Ray を使用して 1 時間ごとに大量のトレースデータを生成しています。開発者は、フィルター式を使用して、ユーザー指定のカスタム属性で返される結果を絞り込みたいと考えています。

開発者は X-Ray で結果をフィルタリングするために、フィルター式をどのように使用すべきですか。

text.en: Add custom attributes as annotations in the segment document.
text.ja: セグメントドキュメントにカスタム属性をアノテーションとして追加する。

text.en: Add custom attributes as metadata in the segment document.
text.ja: セグメントドキュメントにカスタム属性をメタデータとして追加する。

text.en: Add custom attributes as new segment fields in the segment document.
text.ja: セグメントドキュメントにカスタム属性を新しいセグメントフィールドとして追加する。

text.en: Create new sampling rules that are based on custom attributes.
text.ja: カスタム属性に基づいた新しいサンプリングルールを作成する。

comment.en: Annotations in AWS X-Ray are key-value pairs that are indexed for use with filter expressions. This means that you can use annotations to add custom attributes to your trace data, which can then be queried using X-Ray filter expressions. Annotations are used for values that you want to use for searching, filtering, or creating groups. By adding these custom attributes as annotations, the developer can effectively use filter expressions to limit the returned results based on these attributes.
Annotations are indexed, used for filtering, unlike metadata

B. Add custom attributes as metadata in the segment document. Custom attributes are best added as metadata in the segment document because X-Ray filter expressions can use metadata to filter traces. Annotations and new segment fields are not typically used for filtering traces in this context.
A is the correct answer.

https://docs.aws.amazon.com/xray/latest/devguide/xray-sdk-java-segment.html#:~:text=Annotations%20are%20key%2Dvalue%20pairs%20with%20string%2C%20number%2C%20or%20Boolean%20values.%20Annotations%20are%20indexed%20for%20use%20with%20filter%20expressions.%20Use%20annotations%20to%20record%20data%20that%20you%20want%20to%20use%20to%20group%20traces%20in%20the%20console%2C%20or%20when%20calling%20the%20GetTraceSummaries%20API.
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/xray/latest/devguide/xray-sdk-java-segment.html filter expressions => annotations

If you add annotations with the X-Ray SDK, you can also filter based on the presence of an annotation key or the value of a key. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/xray/latest/devguide/xray-console-filters.html
"user-specified custom attributes" means custom annotations
To filter the results in AWS X-Ray using custom attributes, the developer should add custom attributes as annotations in the segment document.

B. Add custom attributes as metadata in the segment document. Custom attributes are best added as metadata in the segment document because X-Ray filter expressions can use metadata to filter traces. Annotations and new segment fields are not typically used for filtering traces in this context.

question.en:
A web application is using Amazon Kinesis Data Streams for clickstream data that may not be consumed for up to 12 hours.

How can the developer implement encryption at rest for data within the Kinesis Data Streams?
question.ja:
あるウェブアプリケーションでは、最大 12 時間消費されない可能性のあるクリックストリームデータに Amazon Kinesis Data Streams を使用しています。

開発者は Kinesis Data Streams 内のデータに対する保管時の暗号化をどのように実装すればよいですか。

text.en: Enable SSL connections to Kinesis.
text.ja: Kinesis への SSL 接続を有効にする。

text.en: Use Amazon Kinesis Consumer Library.
text.ja: Amazon Kinesis Consumer Library を使用する。

text.en: Encrypt the data once it is at rest with a Lambda function.
text.ja: データが保管された後に Lambda 関数でデータを暗号化する。

text.en: Enable server-side encryption in Kinesis Data Streams.
text.ja: Kinesis Data Streams でサーバーサイド暗号化を有効にする。

comment.en: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/streams/latest/dev/server-side-encryption.html

D. Enable server-side encryption in Kinesis Data Streams. Amazon Kinesis Data Streams allows you to enable server-side encryption, which encrypts data at rest. This ensures that data stored within the Kinesis Data Streams is protected with encryption.

D. Enable server-side encryption in Kinesis Data Streams. Amazon Kinesis Data Streams allows you to enable server-side encryption, which encrypts data at rest. This ensures that data stored within the Kinesis Data Streams is protected with encryption.
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/streams/latest/dev/what-is-sse.html#:~:text=Server%2Dside%20encryption%20is,security%20of%20your%20data.
D is the correct answer.

question.en:
An application is real-time processing millions of events that are received through an API.

What service could be used to allow multiple consumers to process the data concurrently and MOST cost-effectively?
question.ja:
あるアプリケーションが、API を通じて受信する数百万件のイベントをリアルタイムで処理しています。

複数のコンシューマーがデータを並行して最もコスト効率良く処理できるようにするには、どのサービスを使用すればよいですか。

text.en: Amazon SNS with fanout to an SQS queue for each application
text.ja: アプリケーションごとに SQS キューへファンアウトする Amazon SNS

text.en: Amazon SNS with fanout to an SQS FIFO (first-in, first-out) queue for each application
text.ja: アプリケーションごとに SQS FIFO (先入れ先出し) キューへファンアウトする Amazon SNS

text.en: Amazon Kinesis Firehose
text.ja: Amazon Kinesis Firehose

text.en: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams
text.ja: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams

comment.en: Real-time data processing is KDS

This service is specifically designed for real-time processing of large-scale streaming data. Kinesis Data Streams allows multiple consumers to process the same stream concurrently, making it highly suitable for scenarios where you have high-volume data streams that need to be processed in real-time by various applications. It offers high throughput, scalability, and durability for streaming data, and enables multiple applications to process the same stream concurrently, making it the most cost-effective and efficient choice for this scenario.
D is the correct answer.

D. Amazon Kinesis Data Streams. Amazon Kinesis Data Streams is designed for real-time data streaming and allows multiple consumers to process data concurrently and in real-time. It can handle millions of events and provides a scalable and cost-effective solution for handling high-throughput data streams.

question.en:
Given the following AWS CloudFormation template:



What is the MOST efficient way to reference the new Amazon S3 bucket from another AWS CloudFormation template?
question.ja:
以下の AWS CloudFormation テンプレートが与えられています。



別の AWS CloudFormation テンプレートから新しい Amazon S3 バケットを参照する最も効率的な方法は何ですか。

text.en: Add an Export declaration to the Outputs section of the original template and use ImportValue in other templates.
text.ja: 元のテンプレートの Outputs セクションに Export 宣言を追加し、他のテンプレートで ImportValue を使用する。

text.en: Add Exported: true to the Content.Bucket in the original template and use ImportResource in other templates.
text.ja: 元のテンプレートの Content.Bucket に Exported: true を追加し、他のテンプレートで ImportResource を使用する。

text.en: Create a custom AWS CloudFormation resource that gets the bucket name from the ContentBucket resource of the first stack.
text.ja: 最初のスタックの ContentBucket リソースからバケット名を取得するカスタム AWS CloudFormation リソースを作成する。

text.en: Use Fn::Include to include the existing template in other templates and use the ContentBucket resource directly.
text.ja: Fn::Include を使用して既存のテンプレートを他のテンプレートに含め、ContentBucket リソースを直接使用する。

comment.en: A. Add an Export declaration to the Outputs section of the original template and use ImportValue in other templates.
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/walkthrough-crossstackref.html

By adding an Export declaration to the Outputs section of the original CloudFormation template, you can make the bucket name available for other templates to import and use. This allows you to reference the bucket name directly in other templates without the need for additional resources or custom logic.
A. Add an Export declaration to the Outputs section of the original template and use ImportValue in other templates.
A is the correct answer.

question.en:
A developer has built an application that inserts data into an Amazon DynamoDB table. The table is configured to use provisioned capacity. The application is deployed on a burstable nano Amazon EC2 instance. The application logs show that the application has been failing because of a ProvisionedThroughputExceededException error.

Which actions should the developer take to resolve this issue? (Choose two.)
question.ja:
ある開発者が、Amazon DynamoDB テーブルにデータを挿入するアプリケーションを構築しました。このテーブルはプロビジョンドキャパシティを使用するように設定されています。アプリケーションは、バースト可能な nano サイズの Amazon EC2 インスタンスにデプロイされています。アプリケーションログには、ProvisionedThroughputExceededException エラーによりアプリケーションが失敗していることが示されています。

開発者はこの問題を解決するためにどのアクションを取るべきですか。(2 つ選択してください)

text.en: Move the application to a larger EC2 instance.
text.ja: アプリケーションをより大きな EC2 インスタンスに移行する。

text.en: Increase the number of read capacity units (RCUs) that are provisioned for the DynamoDB table.
text.ja: DynamoDB テーブルに対してプロビジョニングされている読み込みキャパシティユニット (RCU) の数を増やす。

text.en: Reduce the frequency of requests to DynamoDB by implementing exponential backoff.
text.ja: 指数バックオフを実装して、DynamoDB へのリクエストの頻度を減らす。

text.en: Increase the frequency of requests to DynamoDB by decreasing the retry delay.
text.ja: リトライの遅延を減らすことで、DynamoDB へのリクエストの頻度を増やす。

text.en: Change the capacity mode of the DynamoDB table from provisioned to on-demand.
text.ja: DynamoDB テーブルのキャパシティモードをプロビジョンドからオンデマンドに変更する。

comment.en: C. Reduce the frequency of requests to DynamoDB by implementing exponential backoff. E. Change the capacity mode of the DynamoDB table from provisioned to on-demand.
What the question said is insert data..so B increase read capacity is not correct.Hence C and E.
It 'inserts' data, so it needs WCUs and not RCUs. So option B is invalid too. C and E are the correct options.
C & E correct
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/HowItWorks.ReadWriteCapacityMode.html

B. This error indicates that the application's request rate is exceeding the throughput that has been provisioned for the table. Increasing the provisioned read capacity units (RCUs) and/or write capacity units (WCUs) for the DynamoDB table will allow it to handle a higher request rate, thereby reducing the likelihood of encountering this error. However, this approach requires careful capacity planning and may increase costs. C. Exponential backoff is a standard error retry strategy that involves progressively increasing the delay between retries when there is a ProvisionedThroughputExceededException. This approach helps to smooth out the rate of requests, giving the table time to accommodate bursts of read or write requests. Implementing exponential backoff in the application will help to effectively manage request retries and reduce the chance of continually hitting the provisioned throughput limit.

B And C: While E could resolve the issue by automatically scaling the capacity, it may not be the most cost-effective solution if the traffic pattern is predictable and can be managed with provisioned capacity and backoff strategies.
CE is the correct answer.

By implementing exponential backoff, the application will automatically retry failed requests with increasing delays between attempts. This helps to spread out the requests over time, reducing the likelihood of exceeding the provisioned throughput. Since the table is configured to use provisioned capacity, increasing the number of RCUs will allow the table to handle more read requests per second. This directly addresses the ProvisionedThroughputExceededException by increasing the table's capacity to handle more concurrent requests.

Answer is C & E: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/Programming.Errors.html ProvisionedThroughputExceededException Message: You exceeded your maximum allowed provisioned throughput for a table or for one or more global secondary indexes. To view performance metrics for provisioned throughput vs. consumed throughput, open the Amazon CloudWatch console. Example: Your request rate is too high. The AWS SDKs for DynamoDB automatically retry requests that receive this exception. Your request is eventually successful, unless your retry queue is too large to finish. Reduce the frequency of requests using Error retries and exponential backoff.

https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/ProvisionedThroughput.html#:~:text=The%20DynamoDB%20console%20displays%20Amazon%20CloudWatch%20metrics%20for%20your%20tables%2C%20so%20you%20can%20monitor%20throttled%20read%20requests%20and%20write%20requests.%20If%20you%20encounter%20excessive%20throttling%2C%20you%20should%20consider%20increasing%20your%20table%27s%20provisioned%20throughput%20settings.
choosing C & E after going through https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/HowItWorks.ReadWriteCapacityMode.html

question.en:
A company is hosting a workshop for external users and wants to share the reference documents with the external users for 7 days. The company stores the reference documents in an Amazon S3 bucket that the company owns.

What is the MOST secure way to share the documents with the external users?
question.ja:
ある会社が外部ユーザー向けにワークショップを主催しており、参考資料を外部ユーザーと 7 日間共有したいと考えています。この会社は、参考資料を自社が所有する Amazon S3 バケットに保存しています。

外部ユーザーとドキュメントを共有する最も安全な方法は何ですか。

text.en: Use S3 presigned URLs to share the documents with the external users. Set an expiration time of 7 days.
text.ja: S3 presigned URL を使用してドキュメントを外部ユーザーと共有する。有効期限を 7 日間に設定する。

text.en: Move the documents to an Amazon WorkDocs folder. Share the links of the WorkDocs folder with the external users.
text.ja: ドキュメントを Amazon WorkDocs のフォルダに移動する。WorkDocs フォルダのリンクを外部ユーザーと共有する。

text.en: Create temporary IAM users that have read-only access to the S3 bucket. Share the access keys with the external users. Expire the credentials after 7 days.
text.ja: S3 バケットへの読み取り専用アクセス権を持つ一時的な IAM ユーザーを作成する。アクセスキーを外部ユーザーと共有する。7 日後に認証情報を失効させる。

text.en: Create a role that has read-only access to the S3 bucket. Share the Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of this role with the external users.
text.ja: S3 バケットへの読み取り専用アクセス権を持つロールを作成する。このロールの Amazon リソースネーム (ARN) を外部ユーザーと共有する。

comment.en: Temporary access to S3 object to external users is Pre-signed URL

Presigned URLs are a secure way to provide temporary access to specific objects in an S3 bucket. By generating a presigned URL, you grant time-limited access to the files without having to alter the underlying permissions of the S3 bucket or objects. You can set an expiration time for the URL, ensuring that access to the document is automatically revoked after 7 days. This method is straightforward and does not require the management of user identities or permissions beyond the scope of the shared objects.
A is the correct answer.
A. Use S3 presigned URLs to share the documents with the external users. Set an expiration time of 7 days.
B is the answear. Presinged URLs can be shared 12 hours max
This appear at 17 Jun exam

question.en:
A developer is planning to use an Amazon API Gateway and AWS Lambda to provide a REST API. The developer will have three distinct environments to manage: development, test, and production.

How should the application be deployed while minimizing the number of resources to manage?
question.ja:
ある開発者が、REST API を提供するために Amazon API Gateway と AWS Lambda を使用することを計画しています。開発者は、開発、テスト、本番の 3 つの異なる環境を管理する予定です。

管理するリソースの数を最小限に抑えつつ、アプリケーションをどのようにデプロイすべきですか。

text.en: Create a separate API Gateway and separate Lambda function for each environment in the same Region.
text.ja: 同じリージョン内で、各環境ごとに個別の API Gateway と個別の Lambda 関数を作成する。

text.en: Assign a Region for each environment and deploy API Gateway and Lambda to each Region.
text.ja: 各環境にリージョンを割り当て、各リージョンに API Gateway と Lambda をデプロイする。

text.en: Create one API Gateway with multiple stages with one Lambda function with multiple aliases.
text.ja: 複数のステージを持つ 1 つの API Gateway と、複数のエイリアスを持つ 1 つの Lambda 関数を作成する。

text.en: Create one API Gateway and one Lambda function, and use a REST parameter to identify the environment.
text.ja: 1 つの API Gateway と 1 つの Lambda 関数を作成し、環境を識別するために REST パラメータを使用する。

comment.en: This approach involves creating a single API Gateway and a single Lambda function. Within the API Gateway, you can create multiple stages, each corresponding to a different environment (development, test, production). Similarly, for the Lambda function, you can create multiple aliases, each pointing to a different version of the Lambda function that corresponds to each environment. This setup allows for clear separation of environments within the same set of resources. It simplifies deployment and management by reducing the number of resources and also provides an easy way to promote changes from one environment to another.
C. Create one API Gateway with multiple stages with one Lambda function with multiple aliases.

API Gateway A stage in API Gateway represents a deployment of your API. You can have separate stages for development, test, and production. Each stage can have its own settings, such as stage variables, custom domains, and caching configurations. Lambda function Each alias can point to a specific version of your Lambda function. This allows you to promote versions through different environments without changing the function's Amazon Resource Name (ARN) in your API Gateway.
C. Create one API Gateway with multiple stages with one Lambda function with multiple aliases.
C is the correct answer.

question.en:
A developer registered an AWS Lambda function as a target for an Application Load Balancer (ALB) using a CLI command. However, the Lambda function is not being invoked when the client sends requests through the ALB.

Why is the Lambda function not being invoked?
question.ja:
ある開発者が、CLI コマンドを使用して AWS Lambda 関数を Application Load Balancer (ALB) のターゲットとして登録しました。しかし、クライアントが ALB を経由してリクエストを送信しても、Lambda 関数が呼び出されません。

Lambda 関数が呼び出されないのはなぜですか。

text.en: A Lambda function cannot be registered as a target for an ALB.
text.ja: Lambda 関数は ALB のターゲットとして登録できない。

text.en: A Lambda function can be registered with an ALB using AWS Management Console only.
text.ja: Lambda 関数は AWS Management Console を使用した場合のみ ALB に登録できる。

text.en: The permissions to invoke the Lambda function are missing.
text.ja: Lambda 関数を呼び出すための権限が不足している。

text.en: Cross-zone is not enabled on the ALB.
text.ja: ALB でクロスゾーン負荷分散が有効になっていない。

comment.en: To allow an ALB to invoke a Lambda function, you need to grant the ALB permission to invoke the Lambda. This is typically done by adding a resource-based policy to the Lambda function, granting invoke permission to the ALB. If this permission is not set, the ALB will not be able to trigger the Lambda function in response to incoming requests.
The Lambda function must have the lambda:InvokeFunction permission for the ALB to successfully invoke it.
ANS: C
C. The permissions to invoke the Lambda function are missing.

https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/lambda-functions.html Permissions to invoke the Lambda function If you create the target group and register the Lambda function using the AWS Management Console, the console adds the required permissions to your Lambda function policy on your behalf. Otherwise, after you create the target group and register the function using the AWS CLI, you must use the add-permission command to grant Elastic Load Balancing permission to invoke your Lambda function. We recommend that you use the aws:SourceAccount and aws:SourceArn condition keys to restrict function invocation to the specified target group. aws lambda add-permission \ --function-name lambda-function-arn-with-alias-name \ --statement-id elb1 \ --principal elasticloadbalancing.amazonaws.com \ ....
C is the correct answer.

question.en:
A developer is creating an AWS Lambda function that will connect to an Amazon RDS for MySQL instance. The developer wants to store the database credentials. The database credentials need to be encrypted and the database password needs to be automatically rotated.

Which solution will meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある開発者が、Amazon RDS for MySQL インスタンスに接続する AWS Lambda 関数を作成しています。開発者はデータベースの認証情報を保存したいと考えています。データベースの認証情報は暗号化される必要があり、データベースのパスワードは自動的にローテーションされる必要があります。

これらの要件を満たす解決策はどれですか。

text.en: Store the database credentials as environment variables for the Lambda function. Set the environment variables to rotate automatically.
text.ja: データベースの認証情報を Lambda 関数の環境変数として保存する。環境変数を自動的にローテーションするように設定する。

text.en: Store the database credentials in AWS Secrets Manager. Set up managed rotation on the database credentials.
text.ja: データベースの認証情報を AWS Secrets Manager に保存する。データベースの認証情報にマネージドローテーションを設定する。

text.en: Store the database credentials in AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store as secure string parameters. Set up managed rotation on the parameters.
text.ja: データベースの認証情報を AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store に secure string パラメータとして保存する。パラメータにマネージドローテーションを設定する。

text.en: Store the database credentials in the X-Amz-Security-Token parameter. Set up managed rotation on the parameter.
text.ja: データベースの認証情報を X-Amz-Security-Token パラメータに保存する。パラメータにマネージドローテーションを設定する。

comment.en: B. Store the database credentials in AWS Secrets Manager. Set up managed rotation on the database credentials.
automatically rotated => AWS Secrets Manager
BBBBBBBBBBB
B is the correct answer.

question.en:
A developer wants to reduce risk when deploying a new version of an existing AWS Lambda function. To test the Lambda function, the developer needs to split the traffic between the existing version and the new version of the Lambda function.

Which solution will meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある開発者が、既存の AWS Lambda 関数の新しいバージョンをデプロイする際のリスクを減らしたいと考えています。Lambda 関数をテストするために、開発者は既存バージョンと新バージョンの間でトラフィックを分割する必要があります。

これらの要件を満たす解決策はどれですか。

text.en: Configure a weighted routing policy in Amazon Route 53. Associate the versions of the Lambda function with the weighted routing policy.
text.ja: Amazon Route 53 で重み付けルーティングポリシーを設定する。Lambda 関数の各バージョンをその重み付けルーティングポリシーに関連付ける。

text.en: Create a function alias. Configure the alias to split the traffic between the two versions of the Lambda function.
text.ja: 関数エイリアスを作成する。エイリアスを設定して、Lambda 関数の 2 つのバージョン間でトラフィックを分割する。

text.en: Create an Application Load Balancer (ALB) that uses the Lambda function as a target. Configure the ALB to split the traffic between the two versions of the Lambda function.
text.ja: Lambda 関数をターゲットとする Application Load Balancer (ALB) を作成する。ALB を設定して、Lambda 関数の 2 つのバージョン間でトラフィックを分割する。

text.en: Create the new version of the Lambda function as a Lambda layer on the existing version. Configure the function to split the traffic between the two layers.
text.ja: 既存バージョンの上に新バージョンの Lambda 関数を Lambda レイヤーとして作成する。関数を設定して、2 つのレイヤー間でトラフィックを分割する。

comment.en: https://www.examtopics.com/discussions/amazon/view/88419-exam-aws-certified-developer-associate-topic-1-question-350/

This approach is more efficient and cost-effective than other options such as configuring a weighted routing policy in Amazon Route 53, creating an Application Load Balancer (ALB) that uses the Lambda function as a target, or creating the new version of the Lambda function as a Lambda layer on the existing version
B. Create a function alias. Configure the alias to split the traffic between the two versions of the Lambda function.
B is the correct answer.
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-aliases.html

question.en:
A developer has created a large AWS Lambda function. Deployment of the function is failing because of an InvalidParameterValueException error. The error message indicates that the unzipped size of the function exceeds the maximum supported value.

Which actions can the developer take to resolve this error? (Choose two.)
question.ja:
ある開発者が大規模な AWS Lambda 関数を作成しました。InvalidParameterValueException エラーが発生し、関数のデプロイが失敗しています。エラーメッセージは、関数の展開後のサイズが最大サポート値を超えていることを示しています。

開発者はこのエラーを解決するためにどのアクションを取ることができますか。(2 つ選択してください)

text.en: Submit a quota increase request to AWS Support to increase the function to the required size.
text.ja: AWS Support にクォータ引き上げリクエストを送信し、関数を必要なサイズまで増やす。

text.en: Use a compression algorithm that is more efficient than ZIP.
text.ja: ZIP よりも効率的な圧縮アルゴリズムを使用する。

text.en: Break up the function into multiple smaller functions.
text.ja: 関数を複数のより小さな関数に分割する。

text.en: Zip the .zip file twice to compress the file more.
text.ja: .zip ファイルをさらに圧縮するために二重に zip する。

text.en: Move common libraries, function dependencies, and custom runtimes into Lambda layers.
text.ja: 共通ライブラリ、関数の依存関係、カスタムランタイムを Lambda レイヤーに移動する。

comment.en: C. Break up the function into multiple smaller functions. If the size of the Lambda function is too large, breaking it into smaller, more modular functions can help. Each function can be responsible for a specific part of the application's logic. This approach not only helps with deployment but also aligns with microservices best practices, potentially improving the maintainability and scalability of the application. E. Move common libraries, function dependencies, and custom runtimes into Lambda layers. Lambda layers are a way to manage and share common components across multiple Lambda functions. By moving libraries, dependencies, and runtimes into layers, you reduce the size of the Lambda function's deployment package. Layers can be shared across multiple functions, leading to more efficient use of storage and easier management of common code.
CE is the correct answer.

in this case quota cannot be increased https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/gettingstarted-limits.html#:~:text=The%20following%20quotas%20apply%20to%20function%20configuration%2C%20deployment%2C%20and%20execution.%20Except%20as%20noted%2C%20they%20can%27t%20be%20changed.
C and E
A E no discussion
C and E https://www.examtopics.com/discussions/amazon/view/5330-exam-aws-certified-developer-associate-topic-1-question-17/
A and E https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/gettingstarted-limits.html
A & E is correct
Following anasbakla document, we can see the default storage of Quota is 75 GB for uploaded functions (.zip file archives) and layers.
you are reading the wrong place check the line "Deployment package (.zip file archive) size"

question.en:
A developer is troubleshooting an application in an integration environment. In the application, an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue consumes messages and then an AWS Lambda function processes the messages. The Lambda function transforms the messages and makes an API call to a third-party service.

There has been an increase in application usage. The third-party API frequently returns an HTTP 429 Too Many Requests error message. The error message prevents a significant number of messages from being processed successfully.

How can the developer resolve this issue?
question.ja:
ある開発者が、結合環境内のアプリケーションのトラブルシューティングを行っています。このアプリケーションでは、Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) キューがメッセージを消費し、その後 AWS Lambda 関数がメッセージを処理します。Lambda 関数はメッセージを変換し、サードパーティサービスへ API 呼び出しを行います。

アプリケーションの使用量が増加しています。サードパーティ API は頻繁に HTTP 429 Too Many Requests エラーメッセージを返しています。このエラーメッセージにより、多数のメッセージが正常に処理されなくなっています。

開発者はこの問題をどのように解決できますか。

text.en: Increase the SQS event source’s batch size setting.
text.ja: SQS イベントソースのバッチサイズ設定を増やす。

text.en: Configure provisioned concurrency for the Lambda function based on the third-party API’s documented rate limits.
text.ja: サードパーティ API の公表されているレート制限に基づいて、Lambda 関数にプロビジョンドコンカレンシーを設定する。

text.en: Increase the retry attempts and maximum event age in the Lambda function’s asynchronous configuration.
text.ja: Lambda 関数の非同期呼び出し設定で、リトライ回数と最大イベント経過時間を増やす。

text.en: Configure maximum concurrency on the SQS event source based on the third-party service’s documented rate limits.
text.ja: サードパーティサービスの公表されているレート制限に基づいて、SQS イベントソースに最大同時実行数を設定する。

comment.en: https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2023/01/aws-lambda-maximum-concurrency-amazon-sqs-event-source/

By setting the maximum concurrency on the SQS event source, the developer can control the number of Lambda functions executing concurrently. This approach ensures that the rate of API calls does not exceed the rate limits set by the third-party service, thereby reducing the likelihood of encountering the HTTP 429 error. Adjusting the concurrency settings allows for better control of the throughput to match the API’s capacity.

Option B addresses the issue by configuring provisioned concurrency for the Lambda function. Provisioned concurrency ensures that a specified number of concurrent executions of the Lambda function are always available. This can help in managing the third-party API rate limits by controlling the number of simultaneous requests made to the API. By setting the provisioned concurrency to a value that aligns with the third-party API's rate limits, you can avoid exceeding those limits and reduce the occurrence of HTTP 429 errors.
B. Configure provisioned concurrency for the Lambda function based on the third-party API’s documented rate limits.
At first i also thought of B, but D is more ideal for this scenario. "Restricting" the lambda executions will boost the SQS queue size, its better to put the "restriction" to the SQS itself

It has to be Option B because that is the only correct answer in Q311. Here is the correct answer for Q311, there is no option there for SQS concurrency: B. Configure the REST API in API Gateway to write the requests directly into an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. **Configure the Lambda function with a reserved concurrency equal to the third-party stock application's threshold.** Set Lambda function to process the messages from the SQS queue.

The developer can configure provisioned concurrency for the Lambda function based on the third-party API's documented rate limits. This can help to ensure that the function has sufficient concurrency to handle the incoming messages and make API calls without exceeding the rate limits of the third-party service
D is the correct answer.
this should bring the API in sync with the 3rd part service.

Configuring maximum concurrency on the SQS event source does not directly address the issue of the Lamba function making excessive requests to the third-party service (while it may help indirectly). While Option B: configuring provisioned concurrency for the Lambda function, directly addresses the issue by ensuring that the function scales in a controlled manner based on the third-party API’s documented rate limits.
Provisioned concurrency will not solve the problem as the number of instances can increase till it reaches the max number of unreserved limit (this is not reserved concurrency).

A. increase the batch size does not change how many items being processed. C is from Configuring error handling for asynchronous invocation — You can set it up when creating the lambda. Maximum age of event — The maximum amount of time Lambda retains an event in the asynchronous event queue, up to 6 hours. Retry attempts — The number of times Lambda retries when the function returns an error, between 0 and 2.

question.en:
A company has a three-tier application that is deployed in Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS). The application is using an Amazon RDS for MySQL DB instance. The application performs more database reads than writes.

During times of peak usage, the application’s performance degrades. When this performance degradation occurs, the DB instance’s ReadLatency metric in Amazon CloudWatch increases suddenly.

How should a developer modify the application to improve performance?
question.ja:
ある会社が、Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) にデプロイされた 3 層アプリケーションを運用しています。このアプリケーションは Amazon RDS for MySQL の DB インスタンスを使用しています。このアプリケーションは書き込みよりも読み込みを多く行います。

ピーク使用時に、アプリケーションのパフォーマンスが低下します。このパフォーマンス低下が発生すると、Amazon CloudWatch 上の DB インスタンスの ReadLatency メトリクスが急に増加します。

パフォーマンスを改善するために、開発者はアプリケーションをどのように変更すべきですか。

text.en: Use Amazon ElastiCache to cache query results.
text.ja: Amazon ElastiCache を使用してクエリ結果をキャッシュする。

text.en: Scale the ECS cluster to contain more ECS instances.
text.ja: ECS クラスターをスケールして、より多くの ECS インスタンスを含めるようにする。

text.en: Add read capacity units (RCUs) to the DB instance.
text.ja: DB インスタンスに読み込みキャパシティユニット (RCU) を追加する。

text.en: Modify the ECS task definition to increase the task memory.
text.ja: ECS タスク定義を変更して、タスクのメモリを増やす。

comment.en: Amazon ElastiCache can significantly enhance the read performance of the application by caching frequently accessed data. This reduces the load on the RDS database by serving repeated read requests from the cache rather than querying the database each time. This is particularly effective for applications with a high read-to-write ratio and can lead to a substantial reduction in the ReadLatency metric of the database.
ANS: A
A. Use Amazon ElastiCache to cache query results.
A is the correct answer.

question.en:
A developer is writing an application to encrypt files outside of AWS before uploading the files to an Amazon S3 bucket. The encryption must be symmetric and must be performed inside the application.

How can the developer implement the encryption in the application to meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある開発者が、ファイルをAmazon S3バケットにアップロードする前に、AWSの外部でファイルを暗号化するアプリケーションを作成しています。暗号化は対称鍵方式であり、アプリケーション内部で実行される必要があります。

開発者はこれらの要件を満たすために、アプリケーションでどのように暗号化を実装すればよいですか。

text.en: Create a data key in AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS). Use the AWS Encryption SDK to encrypt the files.
text.ja: AWS Key Management Service（AWS KMS）でデータキーを作成する。AWS Encryption SDKを使用してファイルを暗号化する。

text.en: Create a Hash-Based Message Authentication Code (HMAC) key in AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS). Use the AWS Encryption SDK to encrypt the files.
text.ja: AWS Key Management Service（AWS KMS）でHMAC（Hash-Based Message Authentication Code）キーを作成する。AWS Encryption SDKを使用してファイルを暗号化する。

text.en: Create a data key pair in AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS). Use the AWS CLI to encrypt the files.
text.ja: AWS Key Management Service（AWS KMS）でデータキーペアを作成する。AWS CLIを使用してファイルを暗号化する。

text.en: Create a data key in AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS). Use the AWS CLI to encrypt the files.
text.ja: AWS Key Management Service（AWS KMS）でデータキーを作成する。AWS CLIを使用してファイルを暗号化する。

comment.en: This option aligns with the requirements. AWS KMS allows the creation of symmetric data keys which can be used for encryption outside of AWS. The AWS Encryption SDK is designed to simplify encryption and decryption operations, making it a suitable choice for implementing encryption within the application. The developer can use the data key obtained from AWS KMS for the encryption process.
C and D cannot make it within the application.
A. Create a data key in AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS). Use the AWS Encryption SDK to encrypt the files.
AAAAAA
AWS Encryption SDK can used inside/outside AWS
A is the correct answer.

question.en:
A developer is working on an application that is deployed on an Amazon EC2 instance. The developer needs a solution that will securely transfer files from the application to an Amazon S3 bucket.

What should the developer do to meet these requirements in the MOST secure way?
question.ja:
ある開発者が、Amazon EC2インスタンス上にデプロイされたアプリケーションに取り組んでいます。開発者は、アプリケーションからAmazon S3バケットへファイルを安全に転送するソリューションを必要としています。

これらの要件を最も安全な方法で満たすために、開発者は何をすべきですか。

text.en: Create an IAM user. Create an access key for the IAM user. Store the access key in the application’s environment variables.
text.ja: IAMユーザーを作成する。そのIAMユーザーのアクセスキーを作成する。アクセスキーをアプリケーションの環境変数に保存する。

text.en: Create an IAM role. Create an access key for the IAM role. Store the access key in the application’s environment variables.
text.ja: IAMロールを作成する。そのIAMロールのアクセスキーを作成する。アクセスキーをアプリケーションの環境変数に保存する。

text.en: Create an IAM role. Configure the IAM role to access the specific Amazon S3 API calls the application requires. Associate the IAM role with the EC2 instance.
text.ja: IAMロールを作成する。アプリケーションが必要とする特定のAmazon S3 API呼び出しにアクセスできるようにIAMロールを設定する。そのIAMロールをEC2インスタンスに関連付ける。

text.en: Configure an S3 bucket policy for the S3 bucket. Configure the S3 bucket policy to allow access for the EC2 instance ID.
text.ja: S3バケットに対してS3バケットポリシーを設定する。EC2インスタンスIDに対してアクセスを許可するようにS3バケットポリシーを設定する。

comment.en: C. Create an IAM role. Configure the IAM role to access the specific Amazon S3 API calls the application requires. Associate the IAM role with the EC2 instance.

This approach follows AWS best practices. An IAM role can be created with the necessary permissions to access the S3 bucket. Then, this role can be associated with the EC2 instance. Applications running on the instance can then use the role's permissions to access S3 securely, without needing to manage access keys. This method leverages AWS's built-in security mechanisms and avoids the risks associated with managing static credentials.
Create role with required permissions. Attach it to IAM as instance profile.
C is the correct answer
C is the correct answer
C is the correct answer.

question.en:
A developer created a web API that receives requests by using an internet-facing Application Load Balancer (ALB) with an HTTPS listener. The developer configures an Amazon Cognito user pool and wants to ensure that every request to the API is authenticated through Amazon Cognito.

What should the developer do to meet this requirement?
question.ja:
ある開発者が、HTTPSリスナーを持つインターネット向けApplication Load Balancer（ALB）を使用してリクエストを受け取るWeb APIを作成しました。開発者はAmazon Cognitoユーザープールを設定し、APIへのすべてのリクエストがAmazon Cognitoを通じて認証されることを確実にしたいと考えています。

この要件を満たすために、開発者は何をすべきですか。

text.en: Add a listener rule to the listener to return a fixed response if the Authorization header is missing. Set the fixed response to 401 Unauthorized.
text.ja: リスナーにリスナールールを追加し、Authorizationヘッダーが欠落している場合に固定レスポンスを返すようにする。固定レスポンスを401 Unauthorizedに設定する。

text.en: Create an authentication action for the listener rules of the ALSet the rule action type to authenticate-cognito. Set the OnUnauthenticatedRequest field to “deny.”
text.ja: ALBのリスナールールに認証アクションを作成する。ルールのアクションタイプをauthenticate-cognitoに設定する。OnUnauthenticatedRequestフィールドを「deny」に設定する。

text.en: Create an Amazon API Gateway API. Configure all API methods to be forwarded to the ALB endpoint. Create an authorizer of the COGNITO_USER_POOLS type. Configure every API method to use that authorizer.
text.ja: Amazon API Gateway APIを作成する。すべてのAPIメソッドをALBエンドポイントに転送するように設定する。COGNITO_USER_POOLSタイプのオーソライザーを作成する。すべてのAPIメソッドがそのオーソライザーを使用するように設定する。

text.en: Create a new target group that includes an AWS Lambda function target that validates the Authorization header by using Amazon Cognito. Associate the target group with the listener.
text.ja: Amazon Cognitoを使用してAuthorizationヘッダーを検証するAWS Lambda関数をターゲットとする新しいターゲットグループを作成する。そのターゲットグループをリスナーに関連付ける。

comment.en: This approach uses the built-in capabilities of the ALB to authenticate requests with Amazon Cognito. By configuring a rule action to authenticate with a Cognito user pool, the ALB can handle authentication before the request is forwarded to the target group. The OnUnauthenticatedRequest setting of "deny" ensures that unauthenticated requests are not allowed access, which aligns with the requirement to authenticate every request.
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/listener-authenticate-users.html#configure-user-authentication
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/listener-authenticate-users.html#configure-user-authentication
https://www.examtopics.com/discussions/amazon/view/88889-exam-aws-certified-developer-associate-topic-1-question-332/
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/apigateway/latest/developerguide/apigateway-integrate-with-cognito.html
B. Create an authentication action for the listener rules of the ALSet the rule action type to authenticate-cognito. Set the OnUnauthenticatedRequest field to “deny.”

OnUnauthenticatedRequest The behavior if the user is not authenticated. The following are possible values: deny - Return an HTTP 401 Unauthorized error. allow - Allow the request to be forwarded to the target. authenticate - Redirect the request to the IdP authorization endpoint. This is the default value.

https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/listener-authenticate-users.html https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/APIReference/API_AuthenticateCognitoActionConfig.html
This appear at 17 Jun exam
B is the correct answer.
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/apigateway/latest/developerguide/apigateway-enable-cognito-user-pool.html
I think its C - API G would work better ?

question.en:
A company recently deployed an AWS Lambda function. A developer notices an increase in the function throttle metrics in Amazon CloudWatch.

What are the MOST operationally efficient solutions to reduce the function throttling? (Choose two.)
question.ja:
ある企業がAWS Lambda関数を最近デプロイしました。開発者は、Amazon CloudWatchで関数のスロットリングメトリクスが増加していることに気づきました。

関数のスロットリングを減らすための、最も運用効率が高いソリューションはどれですか。（2つ選択）

text.en: Migrate the function to Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS).
text.ja: 関数をAmazon Elastic Kubernetes Service（Amazon EKS）に移行する。

text.en: Increase the maximum age of events in Lambda.
text.ja: Lambdaのイベントの最大経過時間（maximum age of events）を増やす。

text.en: Increase the function’s reserved concurrency.
text.ja: 関数のプロビジョンド同時実行数（reserved concurrency）を増やす。

text.en: Add the lambda:GetFunctionConcurrency action to the execution role.
text.ja: 実行ロールにlambda:GetFunctionConcurrencyアクションを追加する。

text.en: Request a service quota change for increased concurrency.
text.ja: 同時実行数を増やすためのサービスクォータの変更をリクエストする。

comment.en: C. Increase the function’s reserved concurrency. Reserved concurrency is a feature in AWS Lambda that allows you to allocate a specific amount of concurrency to a particular function. This ensures that the function has a dedicated amount of concurrency and is not affected by throttling due to high usage of other functions in your account. Increasing the reserved concurrency can help mitigate throttling issues, especially if the throttling is due to reaching account-level concurrency limits. E. Request a service quota change for increased concurrency. AWS Lambda has default service quotas (formerly known as limits) for the maximum number of concurrent executions across all functions in your account. If your Lambda function is experiencing throttling due to reaching these account-level concurrency limits, requesting an increase in the service quota for Lambda concurrency can provide a solution.
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/gettingstarted-limits.html#compute-and-storage https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/monitoring-metrics.html
Following issue throttling. C and E is suitable
The correct answer is C&E.

C. Increase the function’s reserved concurrency: Reserved concurrency ensures that a specific number of concurrent executions are always available for your function. E. Request a service quota change for increased concurrency: If your application is experiencing throttling and the reserved concurrency isn't sufficient, you can request a service quota increase for additional concurrency.
CE is the correct answer.

question.en:
A company is creating a REST service using an Amazon API Gateway with AWS Lambda integration. The service must run different versions for testing purposes.

What would be the BEST way to accomplish this?
question.ja:
ある企業が、Amazon API GatewayとAWS Lambdaを統合してRESTサービスを作成しています。このサービスは、テスト目的で異なるバージョンを実行できる必要があります。

これを実現するための最良の方法は何ですか。

text.en: Use an X-Version header to denote which version is being called and pass that header to the Lambda function(s).
text.ja: X-Versionヘッダーを使用して呼び出されているバージョンを示し、そのヘッダーをLambda関数に渡す。

text.en: Create an API Gateway Lambda authorizer to route API clients to the correct API version.
text.ja: APIクライアントを正しいAPIバージョンにルーティングするためのAPI Gateway Lambdaオーソライザーを作成する。

text.en: Create an API Gateway resource policy to isolate versions and provide context to the Lambda function(s).
text.ja: バージョンを分離し、Lambda関数にコンテキストを提供するためのAPI Gatewayリソースポリシーを作成する。

text.en: Deploy the API versions as unique stages with unique endpoints and use stage variables to provide further context.
text.ja: 各APIバージョンを一意のエンドポイントを持つ一意のステージとしてデプロイし、ステージ変数を使用してさらにコンテキストを提供する。

comment.en: Creating unique stages for different versions is a common practice for managing and deploying different versions of REST APIs. => D
D. Deploy the API versions as unique stages with unique endpoints and use stage variables to provide further context.

API Gateway allows you to create different stages for your API, each with its own configuration. These stages can represent different versions of your API (like development, test, and production). You can deploy your API to these stages and have different configurations for each stage, such as different Lambda functions, stage variables, or settings. This approach is straightforward and aligns with best practices for managing different environments in API Gateway.

https://docs.aws.amazon.com/apigateway/latest/developerguide/stage-variables.html#:~:text=With%20deployment%20stages%20in%20API%20Gateway%2C%20you%20can%20manage%20multiple%20release%20stages%20for%20each%20API%2C%20such%20as%20alpha%2C%20beta%2C%20and%20production.%20Using%20stage%20variables%20you%20can%20configure%20an%20API%20deployment%20stage%20to%20interact%20with%20different%20backend%20endpoints.
D is the correct answer.

question.en:
A company is using AWS CodePipeline to deliver one of its applications. The delivery pipeline is triggered by changes to the main branch of an AWS CodeCommit repository and uses AWS CodeBuild to implement the test and build stages of the process and AWS CodeDeploy to deploy the application.

The pipeline has been operating successfully for several months and there have been no modifications. Following a recent change to the application’s source code, AWS CodeDeploy has not deployed the updated application as expected.

What are the possible causes? (Choose two.)
question.ja:
ある企業が、アプリケーションの配信にAWS CodePipelineを使用しています。デリバリーパイプラインは、AWS CodeCommitリポジトリのmainブランチの変更によってトリガーされ、テストとビルドのステージにはAWS CodeBuildを使用し、アプリケーションのデプロイにはAWS CodeDeployを使用しています。

このパイプラインは数か月間正常に稼働しており、変更は加えられていません。最近アプリケーションのソースコードに変更が加えられた後、AWS CodeDeployが期待通りに更新されたアプリケーションをデプロイしませんでした。

考えられる原因は何ですか。（2つ選択）

text.en: The change was not made in the main branch of the AWS CodeCommit repository.
text.ja: その変更がAWS CodeCommitリポジトリのmainブランチに対して行われなかった。

text.en: One of the earlier stages in the pipeline failed and the pipeline has terminated.
text.ja: パイプラインの以前のステージのいずれかが失敗し、パイプラインが終了した。

text.en: One of the Amazon EC2 instances in the company’s AWS CodePipeline cluster is inactive.
text.ja: 企業のAWS CodePipelineクラスター内のAmazon EC2インスタンスのいずれかが非アクティブになっている。

text.en: The AWS CodePipeline is incorrectly configured and is not invoking AWS CodeDeploy.
text.ja: AWS CodePipelineの設定が誤っており、AWS CodeDeployを呼び出していない。

text.en: AWS CodePipeline does not have permissions to access AWS CodeCommit.
text.ja: AWS CodePipelineがAWS CodeCommitにアクセスする権限を持っていない。

comment.en: A. The change was not made in the main branch of the AWS CodeCommit repository: In this pipeline setup, if the change was made in a branch other than the main branch, it would not trigger the pipeline, and therefore, AWS CodeDeploy wouldn't deploy the updated application. B. One of the earlier stages in the pipeline failed and the pipeline has terminated: If one of the preceding stages in the pipeline failed, it would prevent the subsequent stages, including AWS CodeDeploy, from being executed.

A. The change was not made in the main branch of the AWS CodeCommit repository. If the change to the application's source code was not made in the main branch (or the branch that triggers the pipeline), AWS CodePipeline would not detect the change and therefore would not initiate the deployment process. It's crucial that changes are made in the correct branch that is configured to trigger the pipeline. B. One of the earlier stages in the pipeline failed and the pipeline has terminated. If any stage in AWS CodePipeline fails (such as a failure in the test or build stages in AWS CodeBuild), the pipeline stops and does not proceed to the deployment stage. It is common for pipelines to be configured to halt on failure to prevent the deployment of potentially faulty code.

AB the key word is "The pipeline has been operating successfully for several months and there have been no modifications." So the "D. The codePipeline is incorrectly configured" is incorrect, because if the configuration is incorrect, it won't have been operating successfully for several months.

AB is correct - there have been no changes, so someone either merged code into the wrong branch (not triggering the pipeline) or it failed at an earlier stage. Other options dont make sence given the scenario
B. One of the earlier stages in the pipeline failed and the pipeline has terminated. D. The AWS CodePipeline is incorrectly configured and is not invoking AWS CodeDeploy.
AB is the correct answer.
The delivery pipeline is triggered by changes to the main branch - so new code change should have triggered this but this cause errors for some reasons and option C
B. One of the earlier stages in the pipeline failed and the pipeline has terminated. D. The AWS CodePipeline is incorrectly configured and is not invoking AWS CodeDeploy.

question.en:
A developer is building a serverless application by using AWS Serverless Application Model (AWS SAM) on multiple AWS Lambda functions. When the application is deployed, the developer wants to shift 10% of the traffic to the new deployment of the application for the first 10 minutes after deployment. If there are no issues, all traffic must switch over to the new version.

Which change to the AWS SAM template will meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある開発者が、複数のAWS Lambda関数を使用してAWS Serverless Application Model（AWS SAM）でサーバーレスアプリケーションを構築しています。アプリケーションがデプロイされる際、開発者はデプロイ後の最初の10分間、トラフィックの10%を新しいデプロイバージョンに切り替えたいと考えています。問題がなければ、すべてのトラフィックを新しいバージョンに切り替える必要があります。

これらの要件を満たすために、AWS SAMテンプレートにどのような変更を加えればよいですか。

text.en: Set the Deployment Preference Type to Canary10Percent10Minutes. Set the AutoPublishAlias property to the Lambda alias.
text.ja: Deployment Preference TypeをCanary10Percent10Minutesに設定する。AutoPublishAliasプロパティをLambdaエイリアスに設定する。

text.en: Set the Deployment Preference Type to Linear10PercentEvery10Minutes. Set AutoPublishAlias property to the Lambda alias.
text.ja: Deployment Preference TypeをLinear10PercentEvery10Minutesに設定する。AutoPublishAliasプロパティをLambdaエイリアスに設定する。

text.en: Set the Deployment Preference Type to Canary10Percent10Minutes. Set the PreTraffic and PostTraffic properties to the Lambda alias.
text.ja: Deployment Preference TypeをCanary10Percent10Minutesに設定する。PreTrafficおよびPostTrafficプロパティをLambdaエイリアスに設定する。

text.en: Set the Deployment Preference Type to Linear10PercentEvery10Minutes. Set PreTraffic and PostTraffic properties to the Lambda alias.
text.ja: Deployment Preference TypeをLinear10PercentEvery10Minutesに設定する。PreTrafficおよびPostTrafficプロパティをLambdaエイリアスに設定する。

comment.en: C should be it. Shift traffic in two batches is Canary Validation is done with hooks https://docs.aws.amazon.com/serverless-application-model/latest/developerguide/automating-updates-to-serverless-apps.html

This configuration specifies a canary deployment strategy where 10% of the traffic is directed to the new deployment for the first 10 minutes after deployment. If there are no issues, all traffic will automatically switch over to the new version. Setting the AutoPublishAlias property to the Lambda alias ensures that the specified alias is automatically updated after the canary period.
AutoPublishAlias is a requirement. Pre and Post traffic handlers are nice to have

This configuration will ensure that during deployment, 10% of the traffic is shifted to the new version for 10 minutes as a "canary" release. If no issues are detected during this period, AWS SAM will automatically shift the rest of the traffic to the new version. The AutoPublishAlias property is used to specify the alias that the AWS SAM deployment process will update to point to the new version of the function.

Answer is A: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/serverless-application-model/latest/developerguide/automating-updates-to-serverless-apps.html When deploying a Lambda function gradually, CodeDeploy requires a previously deployed function version to shift traffic from. Therefore, your first deployment should be accomplished in two steps: Step 1: Deploy your Lambda function and automatically create aliases with AutoPublishAlias. Step 2: Perform your gradual deployment with DeploymentPreference.
I go with A!
set the Deployment Preference Type to Canary10Percent10Minutes and set the AutoPublishAlias property to the Lambda alias.
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/serverless-application-model/latest/developerguide/automating-updates-to-serverless-apps.html
A
A. Set the Deployment Preference Type to Canary10Percent10Minutes. Set the AutoPublishAlias property to the Lambda alias. Most Voted

A is the answer. The PreTraffic and PostTraffic properties are used for specifying validation Lambda functions to run before and after traffic shifting, respectively. They are not required for the basic canary deployment scenario described.
MyFunction: Type: AWS::Serverless::Function Properties: # ... other properties ... AutoPublishAlias: live DeploymentPreference: Type: Canary10Percent10Minutes

question.en:
An AWS Lambda function is running in a company’s shared AWS account. The function needs to perform an additional ec2:DescribeInstances action that is directed at the company’s development accounts. A developer must configure the required permissions across the accounts.

How should the developer configure the permissions to adhere to the principle of least privilege?
question.ja:
AWS Lambda関数が、企業の共有AWSアカウントで実行されています。この関数は、企業の開発用アカウントに対してさらにec2:DescribeInstancesアクションを実行する必要があります。開発者は、アカウント間で必要な権限を設定する必要があります。

最小権限の原則に従うために、開発者はどのように権限を設定すべきですか。

text.en: Create an IAM role in the shared account. Add the ec2:DescribeInstances permission to the role. Establish a trust relationship between the development accounts for this role. Update the Lambda function IAM role in the shared account by adding the ec2:DescribeInstances permission to the role.
text.ja: 共有アカウントにIAMロールを作成する。そのロールにec2:DescribeInstances権限を追加する。開発用アカウントとの間でこのロールに対する信頼関係を確立する。共有アカウント内のLambda関数のIAMロールに、ec2:DescribeInstances権限を追加して更新する。

text.en: Create an IAM role in the development accounts. Add the ec2:DescribeInstances permission to the role. Establish a trust relationship with the shared account for this role. Update the Lambda function IAM role in the shared account by adding the iam:AssumeRole permissions.
text.ja: 開発用アカウントにIAMロールを作成する。そのロールにec2:DescribeInstances権限を追加する。共有アカウントとの間でこのロールに対する信頼関係を確立する。共有アカウント内のLambda関数のIAMロールに、iam:AssumeRole権限を追加して更新する。

text.en: Create an IAM role in the shared account. Add the ec2:DescribeInstances permission to the role. Establish a trust relationship between the development accounts for this role. Update the Lambda function IAM role in the shared account by adding the iam:AssumeRole permissions.
text.ja: 共有アカウントにIAMロールを作成する。そのロールにec2:DescribeInstances権限を追加する。開発用アカウントとの間でこのロールに対する信頼関係を確立する。共有アカウント内のLambda関数のIAMロールに、iam:AssumeRole権限を追加して更新する。

text.en: Create an IAM role in the development accounts. Add the ec2:DescribeInstances permission to the role. Establish a trust relationship with the shared account for this role. Update the Lambda function IAM role in the shared account by adding the ec2:DescribeInstances permission to the role.
text.ja: 開発用アカウントにIAMロールを作成する。そのロールにec2:DescribeInstances権限を追加する。共有アカウントとの間でこのロールに対する信頼関係を確立する。共有アカウント内のLambda関数のIAMロールに、ec2:DescribeInstances権限を追加して更新する。

comment.en: Create an IAM role in the development accounts. Add the ec2:DescribeInstances permission to the role. Establish a trust relationship with the shared account for this role. Update the Lambda function IAM role in the shared account by adding the iam:AssumeRole permissions.

B To enable cross account AWS service actions, create role with required permissions in account which holds the resource. Enable trust relationship with account that will access the resource. Allow accessing account to assume the role.
Establish a trust relationship with the shared account for this role. Update the Lambda function IAM role in the shared account by adding the iam:AssumeRole permissions.
By using iam:AssumeRole, AWS allows you to implement the principle of least privilege, which means entities have only the permissions they require to perform specific tasks and nothing more.
B is the correct answer.
Classic case of cross account access (CAA)
iam:AssumeRole doesn't exist it is sts:AssumeRole & creating IAM roles within development accounts is unnecessary work

B. Create an IAM role in the development accounts. Add the ec2:DescribeInstances permission to the role. Establish a trust relationship with the shared account for this role. Update the Lambda function IAM role in the shared account by adding the iam:AssumeRole permissions.

question.en:
A developer is building a new application that will be deployed on AWS. The developer has created an AWS CodeCommit repository for the application. The developer has initialized a new project for the application by invoking the AWS Cloud Development Kit (AWS CDK) cdk init command.

The developer must write unit tests for the infrastructure as code (IaC) templates that the AWS CDK generates. The developer also must run a validation tool across all constructs in the CDK application to ensure that critical security configurations are activated.

Which combination of actions will meet these requirements with the LEAST development overhead? (Choose two.)
question.ja:
ある開発者が、AWSにデプロイする新しいアプリケーションを構築しています。開発者は、このアプリケーション用にAWS CodeCommitリポジトリを作成しました。開発者は、cdk initコマンドを呼び出すことで、AWS Cloud Development Kit（AWS CDK）を使用してアプリケーションの新しいプロジェクトを初期化しました。

開発者は、AWS CDKが生成するInfrastructure as Code（IaC）テンプレートに対するユニットテストを書く必要があります。また、重要なセキュリティ設定が有効になっていることを確認するために、CDKアプリケーション内のすべてのコンストラクトに対して検証ツールを実行する必要もあります。

開発の手間を最小限にしてこれらの要件を満たすアクションの組み合わせはどれですか。（2つ選択）

text.en: Use a unit testing framework to write custom unit tests against the cdk.out file that the AWS CDK generates. Run the unit tests in a continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipeline that is invoked after any commit to the repository.
text.ja: ユニットテストフレームワークを使用して、AWS CDKが生成するcdk.outファイルに対するカスタムユニットテストを書く。リポジトリへのコミット後に呼び出される継続的インテグレーション/継続的デリバリー（CI/CD）パイプラインでユニットテストを実行する。

text.en: Use the CDK assertions module to integrate unit tests with the application. Run the unit tests in a continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipeline that is invoked after any commit to the repository.
text.ja: CDK assertionsモジュールを使用して、アプリケーションにユニットテストを統合する。リポジトリへのコミット後に呼び出される継続的インテグレーション/継続的デリバリー（CI/CD）パイプラインでユニットテストを実行する。

text.en: Use the CDK runtime context to set key-value pairs that must be present in the cdk.out file that the AWS CDK generates. Fail the stack synthesis if any violations are present.
text.ja: CDKランタイムコンテキストを使用して、AWS CDKが生成するcdk.outファイルに存在する必要があるキーと値のペアを設定する。違反があればスタックの合成（synthesis）を失敗させる。

text.en: Write a script that searches the application for specific key configuration strings. Configure the script to produce a report of any security violations.
text.ja: 特定の重要な設定文字列をアプリケーション内で検索するスクリプトを書く。セキュリティ違反のレポートを生成するようにスクリプトを設定する。

text.en: Use the CDK Aspects class to create custom rules to apply to the CDK application. Fall the stack synthesis if any violations are present.
text.ja: CDK Aspectsクラスを使用して、CDKアプリケーションに適用するカスタムルールを作成する。違反があればスタックの合成（synthesis）を失敗させる。

comment.en: B. The AWS CDK assertions library provides a convenient way to write unit tests for CDK applications. It allows developers to assert various aspects of the CDK constructs, such as properties and resource counts. Integrating these unit tests into the CI/CD pipeline ensures that they are automatically run after any commit, providing continuous validation of the infrastructure code. E. Aspects in AWS CDK are a way to apply operations to all constructs in a CDK app or a part of the app. By using the Aspects class, the developer can create custom rules (like security configuration checks) and apply them across all constructs in the CDK application. If these rules find any violations, the stack synthesis can be set to fail, ensuring that the application adheres to critical security configurations.
B: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cdk/v2/guide/testing.html fine-grained assertions E: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cdk/v2/guide/aspects.html

B. Use the CDK assertions module to integrate unit tests with the application. Run the unit tests in a continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipeline that is invoked after any commit to the repository. E. Use the CDK Aspects class to create custom rules to apply to the CDK application. Fail the stack synthesis if any violations are present.
BE is the correct answer.

Option B: The standard approach to testing AWS CDK apps uses the AWS CDK's assertions module https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cdk/v2/guide/testing.html Option E: By using the CDK Aspects class to create custom rules, you can enforce specific conditions or checks on your CDK application, including security configurations. Failing the stack synthesis if any violations are present ensures that deployments do not proceed if critical security configurations are not met. CHATGPT 3.5

Option B allows writing unit tests for the infrastructure code using the built-in CDK assertions. Running them in a CI/CD pipeline on commits provides automated testing. Option E lets you define security validation rules as Aspects, which run on synth to catch issues early.

question.en:
An online sales company is developing a serverless application that runs on AWS. The application uses an AWS Lambda function that calculates order success rates and stores the data in an Amazon DynamoDB table. A developer wants an efficient way to invoke the Lambda function every 15 minutes.

Which solution will meet this requirement with the LEAST development effort?
question.ja:
あるオンライン販売会社が、AWS上で動作するサーバーレスアプリケーションを開発しています。このアプリケーションは、注文成功率を計算し、そのデータをAmazon DynamoDBテーブルに保存するAWS Lambda関数を使用しています。開発者は、このLambda関数を15分ごとに効率的に呼び出す方法を求めています。

最小の開発努力でこの要件を満たすソリューションはどれですか。

text.en: Create an Amazon EventBridge rule that has a rate expression that will run the rule every 15 minutes. Add the Lambda function as the target of the EventBridge rule.
text.ja: 15分ごとにルールを実行するレート式を持つAmazon EventBridgeルールを作成する。そのEventBridgeルールのターゲットとしてLambda関数を追加する。

text.en: Create an AWS Systems Manager document that has a script that will invoke the Lambda function on Amazon EC2. Use a Systems Manager Run Command task to run the shell script every 15 minutes.
text.ja: Amazon EC2上でLambda関数を呼び出すスクリプトを持つAWS Systems Managerドキュメントを作成する。Systems Manager Run Commandタスクを使用して、シェルスクリプトを15分ごとに実行する。

text.en: Create an AWS Step Functions state machine. Configure the state machine to invoke the Lambda function execution role at a specified interval by using a Wait state. Set the interval to 15 minutes.
text.ja: AWS Step Functionsステートマシンを作成する。Wait状態を使用して、指定した間隔でLambda関数の実行ロールを呼び出すようにステートマシンを設定する。間隔を15分に設定する。

text.en: Provision a small Amazon EC2 instance. Set up a cron job that invokes the Lambda function every 15 minutes.
text.ja: 小規模なAmazon EC2インスタンスをプロビジョニングする。Lambda関数を15分ごとに呼び出すcronジョブを設定する。

comment.en: Run Lambda as cron = Event Bridge

option A is the most efficient and least development effort option for invoking the Lambda function every 15 minutes, as it leverages Amazon EventBridge's built-in scheduling capabilities and is fully serverless.

Amazon EventBridge (formerly known as CloudWatch Events) allows you to set up a rule with a specific schedule using cron or rate expressions. In this case, a rate expression of rate(15 minutes) can be used. EventBridge rules can directly target a Lambda function, making this a straightforward and low-effort solution to execute the function at regular intervals.
A. Create an Amazon EventBridge rule that has a rate expression that will run the rule every 15 minutes. Add the Lambda function as the target of the EventBridge rule.
A is the correct answer.

question.en:
A company deploys a photo-processing application to an Amazon EC2 instance. The application needs to process each photo in less than 5 seconds. If processing takes longer than 5 seconds, the company’s development team must receive a notification.

How can a developer implement the required time measurement and notification with the LEAST operational overhead?
question.ja:
ある企業が、写真処理アプリケーションをAmazon EC2インスタンスにデプロイしています。このアプリケーションは、各写真を5秒未満で処理する必要があります。処理が5秒を超える場合、企業の開発チームは通知を受け取る必要があります。

最小の運用オーバーヘッドで、この時間計測と通知を実装するにはどうすればよいですか。

text.en: Create an Amazon CloudWatch custom metric. Each time a photo is processed, publish the processing time as a metric value. Create a CloudWatch alarm that is based on a static threshold of 5 seconds. Notify the development team by using an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic.
text.ja: Amazon CloudWatchカスタムメトリクスを作成する。写真が処理されるたびに、処理時間をメトリクス値として発行する。5秒の静的しきい値に基づくCloudWatchアラームを作成する。Amazon Simple Notification Service（Amazon SNS）トピックを使用して開発チームに通知する。

text.en: Create an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. Each time a photo is processed, publish the processing time to the queue. Create an application to consume from the queue and to determine whether any values are more than 5 seconds. Notify the development team by using an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic.
text.ja: Amazon Simple Queue Service（Amazon SQS）キューを作成する。写真が処理されるたびに、処理時間をキューに発行する。キューから消費し、値のいずれかが5秒を超えているかどうかを判定するアプリケーションを作成する。Amazon Simple Notification Service（Amazon SNS）トピックを使用して開発チームに通知する。

text.en: Create an Amazon CloudWatch custom metric. Each time a photo is processed, publish the processing time as a metric value. Create a CloudWatch alarm that enters ALARM state if the average of values is greater than 5 seconds. Notify the development team by sending an Amazon Simple Email Service (Amazon SES) message.
text.ja: Amazon CloudWatchカスタムメトリクスを作成する。写真が処理されるたびに、処理時間をメトリクス値として発行する。値の平均が5秒を超えた場合にALARM状態になるCloudWatchアラームを作成する。Amazon Simple Email Service（Amazon SES）メッセージを送信して開発チームに通知する。

text.en: Create an Amazon Kinesis data stream. Each time a photo is processed, publish the processing time to the data stream. Create an Amazon CloudWatch alarm that enters ALARM state if any values are more than 5 seconds. Notify the development team by using an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic.
text.ja: Amazon Kinesisデータストリームを作成する。写真が処理されるたびに、処理時間をデータストリームに発行する。値のいずれかが5秒を超えた場合にALARM状態になるAmazon CloudWatchアラームを作成する。Amazon Simple Notification Service（Amazon SNS）トピックを使用して開発チームに通知する。

comment.en: https://www.examtopics.com/discussions/amazon/view/88805-exam-aws-certified-developer-associate-topic-1-question-263/

This approach directly leverages AWS services for monitoring and notification with minimal setup and maintenance. CloudWatch custom metrics can be used to track the processing time for each photo. A CloudWatch alarm can then be configured to trigger when any metric value exceeds the threshold of 5 seconds, sending a notification to the development team via an SNS topic.

The values exceeds then changes the alarm from OK to Alarm. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/AlarmThatSendsEmail.html#:~:text=A%20metric%20alarm%20has,determine%20the%20alarm%20state.
Opton C is wrong: CloudWantch action may not use SES, only SNS https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/AlarmThatSendsEmail.html

A. Create an Amazon CloudWatch custom metric. Each time a photo is processed, publish the processing time as a metric value. Create a CloudWatch alarm that is based on a static threshold of 5 seconds. Notify the development team by using an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic.
A is the correct answer.
Option C is incorrect. CloudWatch alarm should be triggered based on "each photo in less than 5 seconds" not "average"

question.en:
A company is using AWS Elastic Beanstalk to manage web applications that are running on Amazon EC2 instances. A developer needs to make configuration changes. The developer must deploy the changes to new instances only.

Which types of deployment can the developer use to meet this requirement? (Choose two.)
question.ja:
ある企業が、Amazon EC2インスタンス上で稼働しているWebアプリケーションの管理にAWS Elastic Beanstalkを使用しています。開発者は設定変更を行う必要があります。開発者は、変更を新しいインスタンスにのみデプロイする必要があります。

この要件を満たすために開発者が使用できるデプロイの種類はどれですか。（2つ選択）

text.en: All at once
text.ja: All at once（一括デプロイ）

text.en: Immutable
text.ja: Immutable（イミュータブル）

text.en: Rolling
text.ja: Rolling（ローリング）

text.en: Blue/green
text.ja: Blue/green（ブルー/グリーン）

text.en: Rolling with additional batch
text.ja: Rolling with additional batch（追加バッチを伴うローリング）

comment.en: https://www.examtopics.com/discussions/amazon/view/88855-exam-aws-certified-developer-associate-topic-1-question-289/

B. Immutable: In an immutable deployment, AWS Elastic Beanstalk deploys the application version to a fresh group of instances in a new Auto Scaling group. Once the new instances pass health checks, they are moved to the existing Auto Scaling group, and the old instances are terminated. This approach ensures that new instances are used for the deployment, minimizing the impact on the existing environment. D. Blue/Green: Blue/green deployment involves deploying the new version of the application to a separate environment (the "green" environment). Once the new environment is ready and tested, the traffic is switched from the old environment (the "blue" environment) to the new one. This type of deployment is effective for ensuring that the new version is deployed on new instances and provides a straightforward way to rollback if needed.

Immutable: An immutable deployment launches a full set of new instances running the updated version of the application alongside the existing instances. It ensures that the old instances remain untouched until the new ones pass health checks. Once validated, the old instances are terminated, resulting in minimal downtime. Rolling with additional batch: In a rolling deployment with an additional batch, Elastic Beanstalk launches a new batch of instances before taking any existing instances out of service. This maintains full capacity during deployments. After successful deployment, the additional batch of instances is terminated.
BD - https://www.examtopics.com/discussions/amazon/view/88855-exam-aws-certified-developer-associate-topic-1-question-289/
B. Immutable D. Blue/green
Why BD? As Blue/Green is related to CodeDeploy, must not it be BE?
correct options : B and D
option E is wong: Rolling with an additional batch --> New **and** existing instances

did you check the link: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticbeanstalk/latest/dg/using-features.deploy-existing-version.html there is a table at the bottom: "The following table compares deployment method properties."
BD is the correct answer.

question.en:
A developer needs to use Amazon DynamoDB to store customer orders. The developer’s company requires all customer data to be encrypted at rest with a key that the company generates.

What should the developer do to meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある開発者が、顧客の注文データを保存するためにAmazon DynamoDBを使用する必要があります。開発者の会社は、すべての顧客データが会社が生成したキーで保存時に暗号化されていることを求めています。

これらの要件を満たすために、開発者は何をすべきですか。

text.en: Create the DynamoDB table with encryption set to None. Code the application to use the key to decrypt the data when the application reads from the table. Code the application to use the key to encrypt the data when the application writes to the table.
text.ja: 暗号化をNoneに設定してDynamoDBテーブルを作成する。アプリケーションがテーブルから読み取る際にキーを使用してデータを復号するようにコーディングする。アプリケーションがテーブルに書き込む際にキーを使用してデータを暗号化するようにコーディングする。

text.en: Store the key by using AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS). Choose an AWS KMS customer managed key during creation of the DynamoDB table. Provide the Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the AWS KMS key.
text.ja: AWS Key Management Service（AWS KMS）を使用してキーを保存する。DynamoDBテーブルの作成時にAWS KMSカスタマー管理キーを選択する。AWS KMSキーのAmazon Resource Name（ARN）を指定する。

text.en: Store the key by using AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS). Create the DynamoDB table with default encryption. Include the kms:Encrypt parameter with the Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the AWS KMS key when using the DynamoDB software development kit (SDK).
text.ja: AWS Key Management Service（AWS KMS）を使用してキーを保存する。デフォルト暗号化でDynamoDBテーブルを作成する。DynamoDBのソフトウェア開発キット（SDK）を使用する際に、AWS KMSキーのAmazon Resource Name（ARN）を含むkms:Encryptパラメータを指定する。

text.en: Store the key by using AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS). Choose an AWS KMS AWS managed key during creation of the DynamoDB table. Provide the Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the AWS KMS key.
text.ja: AWS Key Management Service（AWS KMS）を使用してキーを保存する。DynamoDBテーブルの作成時にAWS KMS AWS管理キーを選択する。AWS KMSキーのAmazon Resource Name（ARN）を指定する。

comment.en: This option allows the developer to use a customer-managed key in AWS KMS for encryption at rest in DynamoDB. The customer-managed key offers more flexibility and control over the key management compared to AWS managed keys. When creating the DynamoDB table, the developer can specify the KMS key to be used for encryption.
https://www.examtopics.com/discussions/amazon/view/78943-exam-aws-certified-developer-associate-topic-1-question-23/

B. Store the key by using AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS). Choose an AWS KMS customer managed key during the creation of the DynamoDB table. Provide the Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the AWS KMS key.
B is the correct answer.
https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/database/bring-your-own-encryption-keys-to-amazon-dynamodb/
B is correct, yes it is seems D from the first look but the keyword is a "customer" not "aws" because says the key compony generated

question.en:
A company uses AWS CloudFormation to deploy an application that uses an Amazon API Gateway REST API with AWS Lambda function integration. The application uses Amazon DynamoDB for data persistence. The application has three stages: development, testing, and production. Each stage uses its own DynamoDB table.

The company has encountered unexpected issues when promoting changes to the production stage. The changes were successful in the development and testing stages. A developer needs to route 20% of the traffic to the new production stage API with the next production release. The developer needs to route the remaining 80% of the traffic to the existing production stage. The solution must minimize the number of errors that any single customer experiences.

Which approach should the developer take to meet these requirements?
question.ja:
ある企業が、AWS CloudFormationを使用して、AWS Lambda関数統合を持つAmazon API Gateway REST APIを使用するアプリケーションをデプロイしています。このアプリケーションは、データの永続化にAmazon DynamoDBを使用しています。アプリケーションには開発、テスト、本番の3つのステージがあります。各ステージは独自のDynamoDBテーブルを使用しています。

企業は、変更を本番ステージに反映する際に予期しない問題に遭遇しました。変更は開発ステージとテストステージでは成功していました。開発者は、次の本番リリースで新しい本番ステージAPIにトラフィックの20%をルーティングする必要があります。開発者は、残りの80%のトラフィックを既存の本番ステージにルーティングする必要があります。このソリューションは、単一の顧客が経験するエラーの数を最小限にする必要があります。

これらの要件を満たすために、開発者はどのようなアプローチを取るべきですか。

text.en: Update 20% of the planned changes to the production stage. Deploy the new production stage. Monitor the results. Repeat this process five times to test all planned changes.
text.ja: 計画された変更のうち20%を本番ステージに更新する。新しい本番ステージをデプロイする。結果を監視する。このプロセスを5回繰り返して、計画されたすべての変更をテストする。

text.en: Update the Amazon Route 53 DNS record entry for the production stage API to use a weighted routing policy. Set the weight to a value of 80. Add a second record for the production domain name. Change the second routing policy to a weighted routing policy. Set the weight of the second policy to a value of 20. Change the alias of the second policy to use the testing stage API.
text.ja: 本番ステージAPIのAmazon Route 53 DNSレコードエントリを、重み付けルーティングポリシーを使用するように更新する。重みを80に設定する。本番ドメイン名の2つ目のレコードを追加する。2つ目のルーティングポリシーを重み付けルーティングポリシーに変更する。2つ目のポリシーの重みを20に設定する。2つ目のポリシーのエイリアスをテストステージAPIを使用するように変更する。

text.en: Deploy an Application Load Balancer (ALB) in front of the REST API. Change the production API Amazon Route 53 record to point traffic to the ALB. Register the production and testing stages as targets of the ALB with weights of 80% and 20%, respectively.
text.ja: REST APIの前段にApplication Load Balancer（ALB）をデプロイする。本番API用のAmazon Route 53レコードを変更して、トラフィックをALBに向ける。本番ステージとテストステージをそれぞれ80%と20%の重みでALBのターゲットとして登録する。

text.en: Configure canary settings for the production stage API. Change the percentage of traffic directed to canary deployment to 20%. Make the planned updates to the production stage. Deploy the changes
text.ja: 本番ステージAPIのカナリア設定を構成する。カナリアデプロイに向けるトラフィックの割合を20%に変更する。計画された更新を本番ステージに適用する。変更をデプロイする。

comment.en: Amazon API Gateway supports canary release deployments, which are specifically designed for this type of scenario. By configuring canary settings, the developer can gradually roll out changes to a small percentage of users (20% in this case) while still serving the majority of users (80%) with the current production stage. This approach helps in minimizing the impact of potential issues with new deployments.
D is correct
D is the correct answer.
